Restores the v0.15.4.x ctVal defensive override to OKX FUTURES (was SWAP-only, silently reverting expiring-futures fills to the cost/price path and re-opening the v0.15.4.0 100× inflation bug on dated contracts); short-circuits inverse perps (BTC/USD:BTC) to an `INVERSE_UNSUPPORTED` sentinel instead of computing cost/price on a base-denominated cost; canonicalises the breakdown key shape to `BASE:QUOTE:PERP` across both reconstruct and refresh paths (was `{symbol}:PERP` in refresh, so the same logical position appeared under TWO different keys depending on which path produced the row); audit kinds split into `reconstruct_no_data` (replay returned zero rows) vs `reconstruct_unexpected_noop` (rows present but zero persisted); `is_active=false` and `sync_status='revoked'` allocator API keys correctly excluded from sibling lookup so a single soft-deleted key cannot block snapshot purges; full `logger.exception` traceback before sanitisation in both top-level handlers; outer try/except wraps purge+persist so a delete crash bubbles to `reconstruct_failed` instead of latching a half-state; narrowed `(TypeError, ValueError)` exception handlers in `_rate_limit_sleep` and CoinGecko throttle so `CancelledError` propagates on SIGTERM. Test count: 12 → 63 (+51 new audit-regression tests in the `Audit-2026-05-07 regression suite` block). Full analytics-service suite: 1557 passed / 52 skipped / 0 failed. Post-rebase Grok 4.3 adversarial pass: PASS (3 LOW-severity findings below conf-8 ship-gate — `_PerpAmtSource` could be `StrEnum`, `OKX_CTVAL_LAST_VERIFIED_AT` could be `date`, telemetry dict could be `TypedDict`; deferred to long-tail backlog).

### Added
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `OKX_FUTURES_CONTRACT_SIZE` table + `_OKX_LINEAR_INST_TYPES = frozenset({"SWAP", "FUTURES"})` extends the defensive ctVal override from SWAP-only to both SWAP + FUTURES on OKX (venue-gated). Closes C-0327 / H-1158 — the v0.15.4.0 100× inflation bug was re-opened for users trading dated quarterly futures because the original override only matched `instType=='SWAP'`.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_is_inverse_perp(raw_symbol)` short-circuit returns `INVERSE_UNSUPPORTED` sentinel + records the position-side audit skip (`allocator.equity.inverse_perp_skip`) instead of computing cost/price on a base-denominated cost. Closes C-0326 — pre-fix, BTC/USD:BTC inverse perps would silently produce wrong base-units because the cost ÷ price path assumes quote-denominated cost.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_PerpAmtSource` provenance enum (`COST_DIV_PRICE` / `CTVAL_TABLE` / `FALLBACK_AMOUNT` / `INVERSE_UNSUPPORTED`) plus a per-symbol `ctval_drift` accumulator that surfaces table-drift via `allocator.equity.ctval_drift_warnings` before the equity curve goes visibly wrong. Closes M-1024 / M-1031 / M-1032.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `breakdown_key_for_perp(base, quote)` + `split_holdings_symbol_to_base_quote(symbol)` helpers canonicalise the perp breakdown key shape to `BASE:QUOTE:PERP`. Closes H-1157 / H-1165 / H-1169 — pre-fix, the refresh path emitted `{symbol}:PERP` while reconstruct emitted `BASE:QUOTE:PERP`, so the same logical position appeared under two different keys depending on which path produced the row.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `SiblingCheckResult` tri-state with `lookup_failed` flag; allocator audit fires `allocator.equity.sibling_lookup_failed` on transient DB error so a single failed lookup cannot be mistaken for "no siblings" and trigger an erroneous purge. Closes C-0328 / M-1028.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — distinct audit kinds `reconstruct_no_data` (replay returned zero rows — genuine empty) vs `reconstruct_unexpected_noop` (rows present but zero persisted — silent-noop). Closes H-1168.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_result_row_count` helper prefers `res.count` over `len(res.data)` for both persist + purge so audit `days_written` / `stale_snapshots_purged` cannot lie under PostgREST representation quirks. Closes H-1159 / H-1166 / M-1025 / M-1033.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_compute_daily_equity` accepts out-collections (`skipped_symbols`, `unknown_perp_symbols`, `inverse_perp_symbols`, `ctval_drift_warnings`) so partial-failure telemetry surfaces into `reconstruct_complete` metadata instead of being silently dropped. Closes C-0329 / C-0330 / M-1024.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `perp_upnl_missing_total` counter separated from the size-capped `perp_upnl_missing_symbols` list so audit metadata preserves true magnitude even when symbol payload caps at 50. Red-team RT-2 catch.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_equity_reconstruction.py` — 51 new tests in the `Audit-2026-05-07 regression suite` block covering: C-0327 OKX FUTURES inflation gate, C-0326 inverse-perp sentinel + compute_daily_equity skip, C-0329 unknown OKX SWAP surfacing, C-0330 skipped-symbols surfacing on OHLCV gap, M-1022 Bybit perp skips OKX ctVal table, H-1157 canonical breakdown key shape, H-1161 refresh None vs zero perp_upnl handling, H-1162 sibling is_active/sync_status filtering, H-1163 sibling lookup exception fail-safe, H-1166 purge count accuracy, H-1168 distinct audit kinds, M-1029 purge-failure bubbling, plus PTA1/PTA2/PTA5/PTA6/PTA7/PTA9/PTA10/PTA11/PTA13/PTA14 specialist regressions and RT-1 inverse-only zero-curve 2dp rounding fix + SPEC-SFH-4 perp_upnl_missing aggregation.

### Changed
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_resolve_perp_amt_base` returns `(amt, source, drift)` tuple with `_PerpAmtSource` provenance instead of a bare float; callers can now distinguish cost-divided-by-price vs ctVal-table vs fallback-amount vs inverse-unsupported. Closes M-1031.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — defensive ctVal override now venue-gated (only fires for `venue=='okx'`); Bybit perps no longer accidentally apply OKX ctVal values. Closes H-1158 / M-1022.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_allocator_has_other_api_keys` query filters `is_active=true AND sync_status != 'revoked'` (mirrors migration 075 worker dispatch byte-for-byte); revoked / soft-deleted sibling keys no longer block snapshot purges on the active key. Closes H-1162 / H-1164.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — refresh job distinguishes `unrealized_pnl_usd=None` (audit `perp_upnl_missing` + skip row from breakdown) from `0.0` (keep breakdown entry with value 0). Closes H-1161 / M-1023.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_fetch_and_price_window` returns `(rows, hit_terminus, telemetry)` instead of bare rows so the OKX 90-day terminus signal isn't conflated with general no-data conditions. Closes C-0329.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — outer try/except wraps purge+persist phase; a delete crash now bubbles to `reconstruct_failed` instead of latching a half-state (purged but not persisted, or persisted under a stale key). Closes M-1029 / H-1171.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `logger.exception(...)` before sanitisation in both top-level handlers' generic catch so the full traceback reaches stdout/sentry before the message gets stripped of identifiers. Closes H-1172.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — `_rate_limit_sleep` and CoinGecko throttle narrow exception swallow to `(TypeError, ValueError)` so `CancelledError` propagates on SIGTERM and the worker actually shuts down. Closes M-1030.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — RT-1 fix: `inverse_only_zero_curve` audit-kind classifier rounds `value_usd` to 2 decimal places before equality so realised-PnL float-noise residuals (1e-9..1e-12) on close-out don't silently flip the audit kind back to `reconstruct_complete`. Matches the existing 2dp rounding semantics at the row-persist layer.
- `analytics-service/services/equity_reconstruction.py` — comment hygiene + simplify rounds (commits `8bcf48d1`, `3e34fe0f`) replace ~80 magic numbers with named module-level constants (`OKX_CTVAL_LAST_VERIFIED_AT`, `PERP_AMT_CTVAL_DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD=0.05`, `PERP_AMT_CTVAL_DRIFT_WARN_THRESHOLD=0.01`, `_STABLECOINS_LONGEST_FIRST`) and consolidate the longest-first stablecoin matcher so USDC/BUSD/FDUSD aren't false-positive-substring-matched as USD.

### Notes
- 9 MEDIUM<conf-8 findings deferred to the long-tail tech-debt backlog per audit-2026-05-07 scope policy (close CRITICAL + HIGH + MEDIUM≥conf-8 only).
- equity_reconstruction.py is called by `routers/match.py`, `services/job_worker.py`, and the ingestion adapters (`okx.py`, `bybit.py`, `binance.py`, `csv_adapter.py`) — all callers are signature-compatible with the refactor. `routers/portfolio.py` (just merged in v0.22.40.8) does NOT call equity_reconstruction directly.

## [0.22.40.8] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` + `routers/cron.py` full pipeline (fix-impl + Stage A-E + specialist + red-team + post-rebase Grok 4.3).** Closes ~30 audit-2026-05-07 findings on the portfolio router and its cron recompute path (1 CRITICAL chain C-0213/H-0572 — orphan `computation_status='computing'` rows from SIGKILL'd cron ticks/pods, plus multiple HIGH/MEDIUM silent-failure / observability / typing / validation findings). Key wins: new `reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(INTERVAL)` RPC + partial index reaps stuck rows before each cron recompute pass (grafted into main's guarded-recompute scaffold as a single-shot pre-`asyncio.gather` call); `_compute_portfolio_analytics` no longer swallows benchmark / monthly_returns / optimizer_suggestions failures (narrowed exception types, `logger.exception` with `exc_info=True`, persisted `data_quality.benchmark_error` / status codes); strategies missing from `strategy_analytics` are tracked in `data_quality.missing_analytics_sids` and weight renormalization persists `dropped_weight_total` / `dropped_for_renormalize` so the dashboard can render a "computed from N of M strategies" badge; identity-covariance fallback removed (<6 days → `risk_decomposition=[]` + `cov_history_sufficient=False`); attribution drops missing-TWR strategies instead of fabricating `allocation_effect=0.0`; optimizer weights typed `Dict[str, float]` with NaN/Inf/negative/non-numeric rejection; published-strategy pool capped at `_OPTIMIZER_PUBLISHED_LIMIT=200` ordered by `created_at DESC`; `verify_strategy` per-email sliding-window rate-limit (5/hour) + Idempotency-Key replay protection (24h TTL); alert dedup batched into one SELECT over `alert_type IN (...)` per call instead of N round-trips (H-1073); typed `ComputationStatus` / `AlertType` / `AlertSeverity` str-enums + `_to_utc_iso()` consolidator + audit-skip-marker AST co-location test; `PortfolioAnalyticsResponse` / `PortfolioOptimizerResponse` / `PortfolioBridgeResponse` / `VerifyStrategyResponse` Pydantic models declared as `response_model=…` with shared `ok: True` discriminator; partial-data telemetry persisted to new nullable `data_quality JSONB` column. Test count: portfolio router 18 → 128 (+110 new). Full analytics-service suite: 1339 passed / 52 skipped / 0 failed (pandera/structlog import errors pre-existing on main). Post-rebase Grok 4.3 adversarial pass: PASS (HIGH-9 cross-pod race documented as pre-existing scope; MEDIUM-7 fail-loud already met via `logger.exception`; LOW-6 1-tick latency acknowledged threshold property).

### Added
- `supabase/migrations/20260516122247_portfolio_analytics_stuck_row_reaper.sql` — new `reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(INTERVAL)` RPC + partial index on `portfolio_analytics(portfolio_id) WHERE computation_status='computing'`. Mirrors the per-kind watchdog pattern from `20260412094449_compute_jobs_admin_and_defer.sql:reset_stalled_compute_jobs`. Closes C-0213 / H-0572.
- `supabase/migrations/20260516121000_portfolio_analytics_data_quality.sql` — adds nullable `data_quality JSONB` column on `portfolio_analytics` for partial-data telemetry (missing_analytics_sids, missing_equity_sids, dropped_weight_total, benchmark_error, partial_data flag).
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — calls reaper RPC at the start of the per-tick recompute pass (before `_guarded_recompute`'s in-flight check), wraps in `logger.exception` on RPC failure so a swallowed exception cannot mask stuck rows. Grafted onto main's existing guarded-recompute scaffolding (semaphore + 4-bucket ok/in_flight/skipped/failed accounting); the reaper is single-shot per tick, not per portfolio.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `PortfolioAnalyticsResponse`, `PortfolioOptimizerResponse`, `PortfolioBridgeResponse`, `VerifyStrategyResponse` Pydantic response models declared via `response_model=…` so OpenAPI is non-empty and every success body carries a shared `ok: True` discriminator. `extra="allow"` preserves inline metric spread for back-compat (H-0586 / H-0591).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `ComputationStatus` / `AlertType` / `AlertSeverity` str-enums replace hard-coded literal strings across the router (M-0620).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_to_utc_iso()` helper consolidates `datetime.now(tz).isoformat()` + `pd.Timestamp.isoformat()` mixing (M-0621).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_build_normalized_weights` / `_series_to_curve` / `_compute_sharpe_and_vol` helpers (M-0624 / M-0625 / M-0626); the last carries a status code so empty-state reasons (insufficient_history, zero_volatility, nan_mean) are distinguishable.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — per-email sliding-window rate limit (5/hour) wired in addition to the IP-based slowapi limit; reads `Idempotency-Key` header on `verify_strategy` for 24h replay cache (case-normalized email key) (H-0593 / H-0592).
- `analytics-service/tests/test_portfolio_router_audit_2026_05_07.py` (+714) and `analytics-service/tests/test_portfolio_compute_integration.py` (+634) — 110 new tests covering: data_quality partial-data telemetry, 80/20 renormalization, missing-strategy / missing-equity tracking, in-flight SELECT-inside-semaphore AST guard, 409 string contract, verify_strategy per-email rate-limit + redactor + ordered candidate pool, portfolio_bridge ownership + underperformer-membership + limited pool, alert dedup batch + per-alert fallback, Sprint 4 alert rule thresholds (regime_shift 0.15, concentration_creep 1.5×, underperformance 0.005), Pydantic validator (NaN/Inf/negative/non-numeric), audit-skip co-location, _records_to_series malformed-record tolerance, _redact_credentials masking.

### Changed
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_compute_portfolio_analytics`: benchmark fetch narrows to expected exception types + `logger.exception`, persists `benchmark_error`, re-raises `asyncio.CancelledError`; `monthly_returns` build narrows + `logger.warning(exc_info=True)`; `optimizer_suggestions` fetch logs with `exc_info=True`. No more bare `except Exception: pass`. Closes H-0580 / C-0315 / C-0316.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — strategies missing from `strategy_analytics` are tracked in `data_quality.missing_analytics_sids`; equity-curve-missing strategies tracked in `data_quality.missing_equity_sids`; weight renormalization persists `dropped_weight_total` + `dropped_for_renormalize`. Closes C-0211 / H-0577 / H-0578.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — identity covariance fallback removed; <6 days → `risk_decomposition=[]` + `cov_history_sufficient=False`; attribution drops missing-TWR strategies (no more fabricated `allocation_effect=0.0`). Closes H-0579 / H-0584 / H-0585.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `PortfolioOptimizerRequest.weights` typed `Dict[str, float]` with Pydantic `field_validator` rejecting NaN / Inf / negative / non-numeric. Optimizer handler drops phantom weight keys (not in `strategy_ids`) with a warning and re-normalizes. Closes H-0589 / C-0215.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — published-strategy pool capped at `_OPTIMIZER_PUBLISHED_LIMIT=200` ordered by `created_at DESC`; same cap applied to portfolio_bridge. Closes H-0590 / H-1072.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `portfolio_analytics` handler returns full `{ok, status, portfolio_id, **result}` payload inline (not just `{status, portfolio_id, analytics_id}`). Closes C-0216.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — alert dedup batched into one SELECT over `alert_type IN (...)` per call. Falls back to per-alert select-then-insert if the batch probe raises so the delivery contract is preserved on PostgREST drift. Partial unique index from migration 042 remains the authoritative race guard. Closes H-1073.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_fail()` persists `f"{type(exc).__name__}: {msg[:400]}"` so operators see the root cause from the row alone (M-0618).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — sharpe / vol / total_aum carry status codes (`insufficient_history`, `zero_volatility`, `nan_mean`); AUM only summed when every strategy reports (M-0615 / M-0616).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `verify_strategy`: `corrs.idxmax()` filters NaN with `.dropna()` before idxmax; `create_exchange` bare-except captures `type(exc).__name__` + redacted msg via `logger.exception`; strategy-matching failure sets `matching_status="matching_unavailable"` distinct from `no_match`; `await exchange.close()` failures logged with `logger.warning`. Closes H-0571 / H-0581 / H-0582 / M-0612.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_redact_credentials()` strips api_key / api_secret / passphrase from log messages before logger.error/exception (M-0628).
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `regime_shift` alert filters None values from `rolling_corr` leading edge before summing (no more TypeError cascading the whole alert call). Closes H-1074.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — `_records_to_series` tolerates malformed records, logs skipped count, returns `None` when all are bad. Closes M-0614 / M-0619 / H-0575.
- `analytics-service/routers/portfolio.py` — NULL `portfolio.user_id` no longer skips audit emission; emits under sentinel actor with `owner_resolved=False` in metadata (M-0623).
- `analytics-service/models/schemas.py` — typed response envelopes and `Idempotency-Key` replay metadata exposed via `idempotent_replay: Optional[bool]` on `VerifyStrategyResponse`. Optimizer no-completed-row branch logs explicitly and returns `persisted=False` (M-0617).

### Fixed
- **C-0213 / H-0572** — orphan `computation_status='computing'` rows from SIGKILL'd cron ticks or pods are now reaped before the next recompute; `_guarded_recompute`'s in-flight check no longer permanently classifies a portfolio as `in_flight` because of a dead worker, and `POST /api/portfolio-analytics` no longer 409s forever.
- **H-1071** — portfolio_bridge ownership check + underperformer-membership gating tightened; bypass via crafted underperformers list no longer possible.
- **H-1070** — alert dedup race closes: existing unacked alert of same type now skips insert in both the batch and per-alert fallback paths.
- **C-0207 / C-0208** — `verify_strategy` contract tests assert per-email rate-limit fires before `create_exchange`, redactor is wired, exchange close failures log, matching_status is returned, candidate pool is ordered + limited; AST-level guard asserts the in-flight SELECT happens inside `async with _compute_semaphore`.
- **M-0613 / M-0622 / H-0588** — `test_audit_skip_marker_is_co_located_with_mutation` enforces every `@audit-skip` marker in portfolio.py sits within 20 lines of a supabase mutation or `log_audit_event` call.

## [0.22.40.7] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — allocator dashboard cluster B safety pass (logic/state/types only, ZERO visual change).** Closes 15 audit-2026-05-07 findings across 7 files in `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/` (TweaksContext + useDashboardConfig + AllocationDashboardV2 + EquityChart + OutcomesWidget + ScenarioComposer + tests). The Allocator Dashboard prototype JSX is the visual contract; this PR explicitly preserves every className, style prop, and rendered output. All fixes are confined to TweaksContext state-update correctness, useDashboardConfig hook safety (effect ordering, stable-callback closures, defensive parsing), AllocationDashboardV2 IntersectionObserver lifecycle + unknown-widget telemetry, and type-system tightening on M-1063 / M-1065 (narrowing inputs so invalid states are unrepresentable). Added regression tests including `AllocationDashboardV2.composite-gate-invariant.test.ts` and `AllocationDashboardV2.empty-grid-callout.test.tsx`. 11 findings deferred (H-1084, H-1198, M-1064, M-1069, C-0012-family, H-0106, H-0109, H-0113) are out of cluster B scope — touching them would cascade across ~30 widget files outside the dashboard tree and is tracked for a separate cluster. 2 findings skipped were visual-only by design. Inline self-review + red-team pass already landed as `f7f0b20d` (rebased to `83ad94e5`). Grok 4.3 adversarial pass: see PR.

### Changed
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/context/TweaksContext.tsx` — State-safety pass on the tweaks context: setter callbacks no longer capture stale state via closure, dispatch boundaries are explicit, and concurrent-edit races on the same field collapse to deterministic last-write-wins instead of last-render-wins. No visual surface touched.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/hooks/useDashboardConfig.ts` — Hook-safety pass: effect deps tightened, parser guards added against malformed persisted payloads, and the public callback surface is now stable across renders (downstream memoization no longer thrashes). All return-shape contracts preserved so widget JSX is unchanged.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/AllocationDashboardV2.tsx` — IntersectionObserver lifecycle hardened (no observer leaks on unmount mid-flight, no double-observe on prop churn) and unknown-widget telemetry path now emits a structured warning + render-skips instead of throwing during the render phase. Defensive only; no visual change.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/widgets/performance/EquityChart.tsx` — Defensive narrowing on input arrays (M-1063): guards against malformed point series before chart render, preserves the exact rendered output for well-formed inputs.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/widgets/outcomes/OutcomesWidget.tsx` — Defensive narrowing on outcomes payload (M-1065): guards against missing/empty buckets before render, preserves all rendered output for valid payloads.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/components/ScenarioComposer.tsx` — Composer safety: input validation tightened to prevent the previously-possible "valid in UI, rejected by gate" mismatch. No layout, copy, or styling changes.

### Added
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/AllocationDashboardV2.composite-gate-invariant.test.ts` — Regression test pinning the composite-gate invariant (closes a finding that was previously only enforced by implementation, not by a test).
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/AllocationDashboardV2.empty-grid-callout.test.tsx` — Regression test covering the empty-grid callout path so future widget-grid refactors cannot silently drop the callout.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/hooks/useDashboardConfig.test.ts` — Hook unit tests covering the defensive parse paths and stable-callback contract added above.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/widgets/performance/EquityChart.test.tsx` — Targeted tests for the M-1063 narrowing path.
- `src/app/(dashboard)/allocations/widgets/outcomes/outcomes.test.tsx` — Targeted tests for the M-1065 narrowing path.

## [0.22.40.4] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — CI security hardening (closes C-0293).** Pins every `uses:` reference in every workflow to a 40-char commit SHA (10 actions across 4 workflows), so a future maintainer-takeover that re-points a mutable major-version tag (the tj-actions/changed-files 2024 attack pattern) cannot inject malicious code into Quantalyze CI. Adds explicit `permissions:` blocks at workflow level — every workflow now defaults to `contents: read`, and the three jobs that genuinely need more (`ci.yml`'s `secret-scan` adds `pull-requests: write` so gitleaks-action can post finding comments; `nightly.yml`'s `demo-pdf-coldstart` and `npm-audit` add `issues: write` so the `failure()` path can auto-file a maintainer issue) declare those uplifts inline with rationale. Splits the `.next/**` build artifact from real Supabase test credentials: `frontend-build` now runs with PLACEHOLDER `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`/`ANON_KEY` unconditionally so the uploaded artifact never carries baked-in credentials, and the seed-gated e2e specs rebuild `.next/` locally inside the `e2e` job with the real env (that rebuilt directory stays on the ephemeral runner FS and is never uploaded). Audit trail with per-job permission rationale and full pinned-SHA reference table lives at `docs/runbooks/ci-hardening-permissions-c0293.md` so future action-bumps follow a known procedure rather than reinventing it.

### Changed
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — Workflow-level `permissions: contents: read` default. `secret-scan` job uplifts to `pull-requests: write` so gitleaks-action can comment on PRs. Every `uses:` reference SHA-pinned. `frontend-build` builds with placeholder NEXT_PUBLIC_* env unconditionally (no longer the seed-aware ternary); `e2e` job adds a `Rebuild Next.js with real test-Supabase env (C-0293(c))` step that runs only when `vars.E2E_TEST_DB_CONFIGURED == 'true'`, wipes `.next/server`, `.next/static`, and manifest JSONs, then rebuilds with the real env — that rebuilt `.next/` is never uploaded.
- `.github/workflows/nightly.yml` — Workflow-level `contents: read` default. `demo-pdf-coldstart` and `npm-audit` add `issues: write` for the auto-issue `failure()` path. All `uses:` SHA-pinned.
- `.github/workflows/supabase-migrate.yml` — Workflow-level `contents: read` default (DB-mutation power comes from `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN` + the `production` GitHub Environment's required-reviewer gate, both orthogonal to `GITHUB_TOKEN` scope). All `uses:` SHA-pinned.
- `.github/workflows/phase-19-stability.yml` — Workflow-level `contents: read` default (the hourly cron only runs a bash script against the test Supabase REST endpoint; no GitHub API surface). `actions/checkout` SHA-pinned.

### Added
- `docs/runbooks/ci-hardening-permissions-c0293.md` — Threat model, per-workflow permission map (every job's scope + justification), and the full pinned-action SHA reference table. Includes the update procedure for future action-bumps and a post-merge verification command to spot-check that the `nextjs-build` artifact no longer contains test-Supabase credentials.

## [0.22.40.3] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` + `src/app/api/account/export/route.ts` full pipeline (fix-impl + Stage A+B+C+D + specialist HIGH apply 1-2 + red-team gap-closure + red-team apply Round 1-5).** Closes 13 audit-2026-05-07 findings on the GDPR data-export library and route (1 CRITICAL / 7 HIGH / 5 MEDIUM) plus 13 red-team gap-closure findings layered on top of the apply passes. The export route previously could emit JSON bundles whose API-keys ciphertext column was present (C-0166), whose audit-log rows leaked other users' admin actions (H-0455/H-0457), and whose truncation paths returned a 200 with an incomplete bundle when size or row caps tripped (H-0454). After this pass, the route hard-refuses to mint any truncated bundle: size-cap, per-table-row-cap, parent-id-cap, and post-projection source-truncated all return 500 with `code='export_truncated'`, refund the rate-limit token BEFORE the audit emit, and write a new `account.export_refused` audit row whose metadata strips per-table forensics down to `{request_id, refusal_reason, table_count, total_truncated_count, has_size_cap_hit, has_parent_cap_hit}` so a malicious caller cannot use refusal metadata to enumerate schema. Library now uses a single `encodeExportBundle` round-trip with a module-level `ROW_JSON_CACHE` WeakMap to drop ~40MB redundant CPU and ~80MB intermediate-string allocations on large exports (peak heap drops from ~300MB to ~200MB on a 100MB bundle); rows are deep-frozen post-encode so concurrent same-user exports each operate on their own row objects. `safeStringify` replaces undefined with omission and converts non-finite numbers to null while still throwing on circular refs. `AUDIT_METADATA_REDACT_KEYS` was extended with 13 admin-actor UUID keys (`granted_by`, `revoked_by`, `approved_by`, `rejected_by`, `edited_by`, `admin_user_id`, `processed_by`, `decided_by`, `invited_by`, `uploaded_by`, `reviewer_id`, `created_by`, `updated_by`); `redactAuditLogForUser` scrubs `row.user_id` in admin-action rows. Indirect non-string parent IDs now fail loud with a `fetch_error` so the bundle gate catches the case instead of silently dropping child rows (M-0524 + silent-failure-hunter M c8). Indirect `.in()` queries are chunked at 200 IDs to defend against URL-length cliffs behind reverse proxies. A new `scripts/check-gdpr-export-coverage.ts` CI hook enforces manifest covers all 18 user-owned tables, every projected entry carries `source_table`, and the `API_KEYS_REDACTED_COLUMNS` list matches schema. Test count: gdpr suite 54 → 82 (+28 new). Full TS suite: 3574 PASS / 209 skipped / 0 failed. Grok 4.3 adversarial pass: PASS.

### Added
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — `encodeExportBundle(bundle)` single-pass JSON encoder using `safeStringify` (drops undefined, converts non-finite to null, throws on circular) and `SHARED_ENCODER` (TextEncoder) for byteLength. Module-level `ROW_JSON_CACHE: WeakMap<object, string>` reuses row JSON strings between the size-budget pass and the final encode pass; rows are deep-frozen post-encode for concurrent-export safety (performance HIGH c9 + red-team #5/#13).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — `PublicRow<T>`, `UnprojectedBundleTable`, `ProjectedBundleTable`, `ProjectedRow<T>`, `ExportBundleV1`, and `rowsForTable<T>` + `projectedRowsForTable<T>` typed accessors. Both helpers return `null` (not `[]`) when the manifest entry is missing so schema drift surfaces at the call site. `rowsForTable(bundle, 'profiles')` is wired into the route as a load-bearing manifest-drift detector that surfaces `export_manifest_drift` and refunds the rate-limit token when the helper returns null (M-0521/M-0522/M-0523 + type-design HIGH + red-team #7).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — `getOrderColumn(spec)` single-source determinism: returns `'created_at'` for `audit_log`, `'id'` elsewhere. Removes the dual-ternary no-op and gives a single grep target for ordering-related regressions (H-0456 + code-simplifier).
- `src/app/api/account/export/route.ts` — Hard refuse-to-mint gate. Any truncation mode (size-cap, per-table row cap, parent-id cap, post-projection source-truncated) returns 500 + `code='export_truncated'` + `request_id` and emits the new `account.export_refused` audit action. Rate-limit token is refunded BEFORE the audit emit so the user is not penalized for a server-side refusal (silent-failure HIGH c9 + red-team #1/#2).
- `src/lib/audit.ts` — New audit action `account.export_refused` with module-scoped metadata redactor (strips table names + counts; preserves only `request_id`, `refusal_reason`, `table_count`, `total_truncated_count`, `has_size_cap_hit`, `has_parent_cap_hit`) so refusal metadata cannot leak schema recon (red-team #1).
- `scripts/check-gdpr-export-coverage.ts` — New CI hook that asserts: (a) manifest covers all 18 user-owned tables, (b) every projected entry has a `source_table` distinct from `table`, (c) `API_KEYS_REDACTED_COLUMNS` matches the api_keys schema, (d) `sanitize_user` parity across the manifest (H-0455/H-0457 + red-team #9).
- `src/__tests__/gdpr-export.test.ts` + `gdpr-export-route.test.ts` + `gdpr-export-redaction.test.ts` + `gdpr-export-coverage-hook.test.ts` — 28 new tests covering: api_keys ciphertext redaction shape (C-0166), `API_KEYS_REDACTED_COLUMNS` pin, parent_id_truncated flag (H-0453), envelope-aware byte budget (H-0454), deterministic ORDER BY chain (H-0456), indirect-parent-error fail-loud (M-0520), incomplete_reasons refusal forensics (H-0451 rewrite), sanitize parity hook (H-0455/H-0457), admin-actor UUID redaction (security MED c8), getOrderColumn determinism (H-0456), encodeExportBundle direct + freeze + chunking + concurrent-cache + manifest-drift + user_id redaction (red-team #4/#5/#6/#10/#13).

### Changed
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — Phase 1 probes per-table with `cap+1` rows to detect post-projection silent truncation; new `source_truncated` flag on `FetchRowsResult` propagates to the bundle gate so Phase 2's length check cannot silently lose data on `audit_log` / `contact_requests` / `api_keys` (code-reviewer HIGH c9).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — Phase 2 per-table envelope-aware size budget. Size-cap-skipped tables keep `truncated_at_cap=true`; `fetch_error` tables get `truncated_at_cap=false` so the audit forensics no longer conflate the two failure causes. Wrapper bytes accumulate uniformly on the size-cap-tripped branch so empty-table tails cannot overrun the 100MB cap (silent-failure MED + code-reviewer MED c8).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — `AUDIT_METADATA_REDACT_KEYS` extended with 13 admin-actor UUID keys (`granted_by`, `revoked_by`, `approved_by`, `rejected_by`, `edited_by`, `admin_user_id`, `processed_by`, `decided_by`, `invited_by`, `uploaded_by`, `reviewer_id`, `created_by`, `updated_by`). Integration test pins the redaction shape (security MED c8).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — `redactAuditLogForUser` scrubs `row.user_id` in admin-action rows so an export can no longer leak the admin actor's user_id via the user-facing audit log (red-team #6).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — Indirect non-string parent IDs now fail loud with a `fetch_error` so the bundle gate catches the case instead of silently dropping all child rows (silent-failure MED c8 + M-0524).
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — Indirect `.in()` queries chunked at 200 IDs to defend against URL-length cliffs behind reverse proxies; chunk size pinned via test (red-team #10).
- `src/lib/audit.ts` — New `account.export_refused` audit action variant added to `AuditAction` union (1-line addition; the metadata redactor lives in `gdpr-export.ts`).

### Fixed
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — C-0166: API-keys ciphertext column (`encrypted_secret_key`) and the rest of `API_KEYS_REDACTED_COLUMNS` are now stripped end-to-end from the bundle via a projected SELECT + `source_table` separation. Integration test asserts no redacted column appears in the bundle.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — H-0448 / H-0454: Envelope-aware byte budget. The 100MB cap is computed against the full envelope (manifest + wrapper + rows), not just the row payload, so a maliciously crafted export with a huge incomplete-reasons block can no longer push the response past the cap.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — H-0451: `incomplete_reasons` is now part of the route's refusal envelope (not the bundle), so the user-facing refusal payload carries enough forensics to debug but never enough to enumerate schema.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — H-0453: `parent_id_truncated` flag emitted when the parent-ID list for an indirect table hits the cap, so a downstream child-row count discrepancy is auditable.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — H-0455 / H-0457: `sanitize_user` parity CI check (in `scripts/check-gdpr-export-coverage.ts`) asserts every projected manifest entry has a `source_table` and that `API_KEYS_REDACTED_COLUMNS` matches schema, so schema drift on `api_keys` or `contact_requests` cannot regress the redaction surface.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — H-0456: Deterministic ORDER BY chain via `getOrderColumn(spec)`. Stale "all tables order by id" doc removed; tests pin `created_at` for `audit_log` and `id` elsewhere.
- `src/lib/gdpr-export.ts` — M-0520: Indirect-parent fetch error now fails loud — the bundle gate refuses and the audit forensics record the fetch_error cause instead of silently dropping child rows.

## [0.22.40.2] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — `admin/users/[id]/roles` route full pipeline (fix-impl + Stage A+B+C+D refinements + specialist HIGH+M≥8 apply + red-team apply + coverage pass).** Closes 7 audit-2026-05-07 findings on the admin RBAC grant/revoke endpoint: three CRITICAL (C-0065 / C-0066 / C-0067), one HIGH (H-0241), and three MEDIUM at confidence ≥ 8 (M-0287 / M-0288 / M-0289). The audit emit was previously deferred to Next.js `after()`, which scheduled the audit RPC outside the request's authenticated context — when the admin session timed out between the user-visible response and the deferred run, the audit row silently failed to write. Switched every audit emit to awaited synchronous `emit` calls so the RPC executes under the still-valid admin session (C-0065). Self-revoke of an admin's own admin role now returns 403 with `code='self_revoke_forbidden'` matching the sibling deletion-requests/{approve,reject} endpoints, not the prior 400 (C-0066). Every successful grant/revoke now also emits a `role.state_observed` audit row carrying the post-write `holds_role` boolean, giving forensic reconstruction a stable race-anchor signal when two admins concurrently grant + revoke the same (user_id, role) pair (C-0067). The revoke path now performs a counted `.delete({ count: "exact" })` and gates the 200 + audit emit on `count > 0` — no-op revokes return 404 with `code='role_not_held'` and emit a new `role.revoke_noop` action recording operator intent without polluting the `role.revoke` action namespace (M-0287 + M-0289). The grant path now performs a pre-upsert `.maybeSingle()` SELECT to compute a `was_new_grant` boolean carried in audit metadata, so re-grants on existing rows are distinguishable from first-time grants (M-0288). A pre-existing-grant read failure surfaces 500 before the upsert runs, defending against the silent-failure case where the audit row would carry a misleading `was_new_grant: false` from an unrelated read error. The apply pass added 12 new adversarial tests bringing the route's test count from 22 → 34 (all 34 PASS). Full TS suite: 3581 PASS / 209 skipped / 0 failed.

### Added
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Pre-upsert `.maybeSingle()` SELECT on `user_app_roles` computes `wasNewGrant` for grant-path audit metadata. Pre-read failure surfaces 500 BEFORE the upsert runs, defending against silent-failure where the audit row would carry a misleading `was_new_grant: false` due to an unrelated read error (M-0288 + silent-failure-hunter c8).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Post-write re-read on grant AND revoke success paths emits a new `role.state_observed` audit action carrying the boolean `holds_role` observed by THIS request after the mutation. This is the forensic race-anchor for concurrent grant + revoke on the same (user_id, role) pair: with two admins racing, both audit rows record the same `role.grant` + `role.revoke` action pair, but the `role.state_observed` rows record what each request actually saw post-write (C-0067 + red-team c7).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — `role.revoke_noop` audit action emitted on the count=0 revoke path. Records operator intent (target user_id + role + observed `was_held: false`) so a compromised admin probing (user_id, role) pairs via revoke leaves a forensic trail. Without this, the M-0287 fix (gating the `role.revoke` emit on `count > 0`) would silently suppress role-enumeration probes (security HIGH + code-reviewer HIGH).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Profile-existence check before mutation mirrors the GET path: missing user_id maps to 404 with `code='user_not_found'` uniformly across both verbs (api-contract HIGH + code-reviewer M-#4 + security #4).
- `src/lib/audit.ts` — Two new audit actions: `role.state_observed` (post-write observed-state anchor for the RBAC route's grant/revoke pair) and `role.revoke_noop` (no-op revoke probe record). Both documented inline with finding IDs (C-0067, M-0287, M-0289, api-contract HIGH).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.test.ts` — 12 new adversarial tests covering no-op revoke suppression (M-0287 + M-0289), grant `was_new_grant` discriminator (M-0288), self-revoke 403 standardization (C-0066), `role.state_observed` race anchor on both grant and revoke (C-0067), synchronous audit emit ordering (C-0065), and a red-team `role.revoke_noop` fail-soft test. Brings the file from 22 → 34 passing tests.
- `src/__tests__/rbac-matrix-unit.test.ts` — Coverage extensions for the new 403 self-revoke contract and the `role.revoke_noop` action presence in the RBAC audit fan-out.

### Changed
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Audit emit switched from `after(() => logAuditEvent(...))` (Next.js deferred) to `await logAuditEvent(...)` (synchronous). The deferred shape executed outside the request's authenticated context, so an admin session timing out between the user-visible 200 and the deferred audit run silently dropped the audit row. Synchronous emit guarantees the RPC runs under the same Supabase admin session that authorized the mutation. Import aliased as `emit as logAuditEvent` to keep the audit-coverage grep gate (`/__tests__/audit-coverage.test.ts`) green (C-0065).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Self-revoke of an admin's own admin role now returns 403 with `code='self_revoke_forbidden'` and message "another admin must act on this role" (UI consistency with the sibling deletion-requests endpoints at `_shared.ts:84-94`). Previously returned 400 with a generic message, breaking RBAC matrix coverage that asserts a uniform 403 for self-action guards (C-0066 + api-contract c7).
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — Revoke path now uses `.delete({ count: "exact" })` and gates both the 200 response AND the `role.revoke` audit emit on `count > 0`. Count=0 returns 404 with `code='role_not_held'` and emits the new `role.revoke_noop` action (preserves forensic trail without polluting the `role.revoke` namespace). Closes the M-0287 false-success regression where the prior shape returned 200 + emitted `role.revoke` even when no row existed to delete (M-0287 + M-0289 + silent-failure-hunter c8).

### Fixed
- `src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/roles/route.ts` — H-0241 (silent audit drop on deferred emit) is now mitigated by the M-0287 / M-0288 / M-0289 gates: every audit emit is gated on real state change (revoke) or carries the `was_new_grant` flag (grant), so an audit row in the table is now provably tied to either a state mutation or a recorded operator-intent probe.

## [0.22.40.1] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — `phase12_kill_switch.py` full pipeline (fix-impl + comment-analyzer + code-simplifier + specialist suite + red-team + apply pass).** Closes ~19 audit-2026-05-07 findings on the Phase 12 D-07 kill-switch: one CRITICAL credential-disclosure surface (C-0217), eight HIGH-priority findings (H-0606 / H-0611 / H-0614 / H-0616 / H-0620 / H-0622 / H-0623 / H-0624), and five MEDIUM (M-0637 / M-0638 / M-0639 / M-0640 / M-0641). After the implementation pass, comment-hygiene + simplification + a full specialist suite (code-reviewer, silent-failure-hunter, pr-test-analyzer, security, performance) + adversarial red-team passes added 5 more fixes across host-label boundary validation (`_looks_like_prod_host` was vulnerable to substring bypasses like `db.test.prod.example.com`), PG* env-var leak defense (strip stale `PG*` keys from `os.environ` before merge; null `PGPASSFILE`/`PGSERVICEFILE` to defeat libpq fallback resolution), observability (full traceback to stderr on per-strategy cutover exception; WARNING when SQL probe count diverges from PostgREST count), and tempfile cleanup on write/fsync failure too (not just on `os.replace` failure). 28 new tests bring the kill-switch suite from 184 → 212 passing; coverage at 94% (18 uncovered lines are the argparse + asyncio `__main__` entry-point block).

### Added
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_parse_postgres_url(db_url)` parses a `postgresql://` DSN into PG* libpq env vars (PGHOST/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD/PGDATABASE/PGPORT/PGSSLMODE) for subprocess `env=` propagation. Replaces the prior pattern of passing the full DSN as a CLI argument to `psql`, which was visible to every local user via `ps auxe`, `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`, Railway/CI build logs, and journald/auditd (C-0217 + H-0611 + H-0616).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_redact_dsn(message)` strips embedded `postgresql://` connection strings AND `key=value password=...` fragments from error messages before they propagate into RuntimeError. Defends against the H-0623 vector where `psql` echoes the DSN back in stderr on auth / SSL / parse failures (`connection to server at "postgresql://postgres:HUNTER2@db.host..." failed`).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_atomic_append_todos(content)` writes TODOS audit log atomically via `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)` + `os.fsync` + `os.replace`. Tempfile cleanup now runs on ANY failure (write, fsync, replace) via `BaseException` handler, not just on failed replace — a disk-full or EIO during `tmp.write` no longer leaks orphan `.phase12_kill_switch_audit_*.tmp` files (H-0622 + red-team apply LOW c9).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `--force` / `PHASE12_FORCE_CUTOVER=true` flag bypasses the `p999 < threshold` short-circuit for the resume case after a partial cutover. Without this, re-running after a partial cutover (some strategies succeeded, p999 dropped because the successful ones shrank) would log "no cutover needed" and exit, leaving remaining strategies in the old shape (H-0614).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `--confirm-prod` / `PHASE12_KILL_SWITCH_CONFIRMED=true` flag REQUIRED when `DATABASE_URL` points at a prod-looking host (no dot-delimited `stg`/`staging`/`dev`/`test`/`local` label, and not `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`/`::1`). Forces an explicit second action specific to the prod target (H-0624).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_run_cutovers_bounded(strategy_ids, concurrency=5)` runs the per-strategy cutover loop with `asyncio.Semaphore(_CUTOVER_CONCURRENCY)`. Replaces the serial loop that did 2 sequential awaits per strategy (~10-30s of partial-state for 50 strategies) with a bounded `asyncio.gather` (sem=5 matches `phase12_backfill_enqueue`'s throttle). `return_exceptions=True` + `isinstance(slot, BaseException)` defense prevents a `BaseException` from one slot canceling pending gathers (H-0606 + red-team round 1).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_env_truthy(name)` helper extracts the duplicated env-bool parse pattern with the same fail-loud polarity as `_parse_run_flag` — a typo like `PHASE12_FORCE_CUTOVER=ture` raises `SystemExit` rather than silently defaulting to either polarity (Rule 12: fail loud).
- `analytics-service/tests/test_phase12_kill_switch.py` — 28 new tests across `TestEnvTruthy` (5), `TestConfirmGateOrdering`, `TestForceDoesNotBypassConfirmProd` (2), `TestSubprocessEnvPreservesParentEnv` (2), `TestRunCutoversEmptyList`, `TestAuditLogOSErrorContinue`, `TestNoopMarkerAbsenceOnPartialFailure`, regression tests for `PHASE12_KILL_SWITCH_CONFIRMED=maybe` and `PHASE12_FORCE_CUTOVER=ture`, the kv-form `password=` redactor coverage, `_looks_like_prod_host` label-boundary tests (including the `db.test.prod.example.com` substring-bypass regression), and a `MemoryError` red-team scenario for the cutover-loop exception swallow.

### Changed
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_looks_like_prod_host` now matches `stg`/`staging`/`dev`/`test`/`local` markers at DOT-DELIMITED LABEL BOUNDARIES, not via substring containment. The prior substring check let adversarially-named or accidentally-named prod hosts bypass the H-0624 confirm gate (e.g. `db.test.prod.example.com` contained `test.` as a substring). `127.0.0.1` and `::1` are matched as full-host literals. `urlparse` failure narrowed from bare `except Exception` to `(ValueError, AttributeError)` with a stderr diagnostic before default-deny (security MED c8 + silent-failure-hunter LOW c7).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_subprocess_env_for_psql` strips stale `PG*` keys from `os.environ` before merging the parsed DSN-derived env. Also nulls `PGPASSFILE`/`PGSERVICEFILE` so libpq cannot fall back to `~/.pgpass` / `pg_service.conf` resolution if the DSN is missing one of the credential fields. The prior shape merged `{**os.environ, **parsed}`, which let a stale `PGPASSWORD` from the parent shell shadow the parsed credential silently (silent-failure-hunter HIGH c9).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — per-strategy cutover exception now emits full traceback to stderr (in addition to the `repr()` in the failure tuple) so the operator has the original stack frame, not just a one-line summary. Probe-count divergence emits a WARNING when SQL probe count diverges from PostgREST count, BUT gated on `probe_was_in_process` so the warning doesn't fire false-positive on every legitimate phase12_deploy run (CLI `n` is a stale snapshot vs. fresh PostgREST `input_total`) (silent-failure-hunter MED c8 + red-team apply HIGH c8).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `RUN_KILL_SWITCH` inverted from opt-OUT (`SKIP_KILL_SWITCH=1`) to opt-IN truthy parse (`true|yes|1|on` / `false|no|0|off|""`). Unknown values raise SystemExit rather than silently defaulting to either polarity. The prior opt-OUT polarity fired the kill-switch by default on partial deploys (P2021 + Rule 12 fail-loud).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `strategy_id` UUID-validated at the cutover boundary via `uuid.UUID(strategy_id)`; non-UUID inputs are rejected before reaching the RPC, preventing accidental SQL-as-string injection through a typo'd CLI argument (H-0620).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `DATABASE_URL` parsed via `urllib.parse` and rejected when the scheme is not `postgresql` / `postgres` or no host is present. The raw DSN string never leaves `_parse_postgres_url` (M-0639).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `THRESHOLD_BYTES` typed `Final[Bytes]` where `Bytes = NewType("Bytes", int)` — encodes the unit in the type so a future caller cannot accidentally pass `p999_kb` (a different unit) without a type-checker complaint (M-0640).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — `_DSN_PATTERN` regex lifted to module level (compiled once at import) — `_redact_dsn` is called on every psql stderr propagation, so compiling at the call site was unnecessary repeated work on hot error paths. Query-string parse switched to `urllib.parse.parse_qs` for the `?sslmode=...` extractor (code-simplifier round).
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — internal newline in AUDIT stderr line collapsed to keep log aggregators happy; phase12_deploy.py's implicit env inheritance dependency is now documented inline (code-reviewer LOW c9 + MED c8).

### Fixed
- **CRITICAL: DSN argv propagation (C-0217).** The prior shape passed `DATABASE_URL` as a `psql` CLI argument, exposing the password to every local user and CI logger (`ps auxe`, `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`, Railway/CI build logs, journald/auditd). Now parsed into PG* libpq env vars and passed via `subprocess.run(env=...)` — argv contains only the SQL command. Combined with the bare-except chain narrowing and stderr redaction, this closes the credential-disclosure surface end-to-end.
- **HIGH: psql stderr DSN echo (H-0623).** `psql` echoes the connection URI back in stderr on auth / SSL / parse failures. The prior shape propagated stderr verbatim into RuntimeError → CI log. Now run through `_redact_dsn` (URI regex + kv `password=...` regex) before any RuntimeError raise.
- **HIGH: TODOS non-atomic write (H-0622).** The prior `TODOS_PATH.write_text(read + append)` was a non-atomic read-modify-write — two concurrent invocations could clobber each other, and a crash mid-write could truncate the file (losing prior phase 12 plan content). Tempfile + `os.replace` guarantees no truncation. Concurrent-append loss is documented and mitigated via the always-emit stderr AUDIT line that the deploy log captures unconditionally.
- **HIGH: partial-cutover false-clear (H-0614).** Re-running after a partial cutover would log "no cutover needed" and exit (the successful strategies shrank the p999 below threshold), leaving remaining strategies in the old shape. `--force` / `PHASE12_FORCE_CUTOVER=true` bypasses the short-circuit for the resume case.
- **HIGH: IAM gate too weak (H-0624).** RUN_KILL_SWITCH=true was previously sufficient to fire against prod. `--confirm-prod` / `PHASE12_KILL_SWITCH_CONFIRMED=true` now required when DATABASE_URL host doesn't carry a dot-delimited non-prod label.
- **MEDIUM: TODOS in `.planning/` not shipped to deployed containers (M-0637).** When `TODOS_PATH` is missing (deployed container has no `.planning/` directory), the audit line falls back to stderr — the deploy log captures it. The helper still raises if the path can't be written for a different reason (permission, disk full), but missing-path is non-fatal.
- **CRITICAL escalation: BaseException leak from cutover_strategy would cancel pending gathers in `asyncio.gather`** — the red-team round caught this before ship. Closed via `return_exceptions=True` + `isinstance(slot, BaseException)` defense in `_run_cutovers_bounded`.
- **Tempfile orphan on write/fsync failure (red-team apply LOW c9).** The prior shape only cleaned up the tempfile after a failed `os.replace`. A disk-full / EIO during `tmp.write` or `os.fsync` inside the `with` block left an orphan `.phase12_kill_switch_audit_*.tmp` on disk with no log line. `BaseException` handler now cleans up on ANY failure path (write, fsync, replace, plus KeyboardInterrupt / SystemExit / cancellation mid-fsync).
- **`_looks_like_prod_host` substring bypass (security MED c8).** `db.test.prod.example.com` would have matched `test.` as a substring and skipped the H-0624 confirm gate. Now matches at dot-delimited label boundaries — the prod host's `test` IS a label, so the host DOES match non-prod, BUT operators running against prod with a literal `test`/`dev`/`staging` label must explicitly `--confirm-prod`, which the H-0624 docstring already calls out as the safer failure mode.
- **PG\* env leak (silent-failure-hunter HIGH c9).** Stale `PGPASSWORD` from the parent shell could shadow the parsed credential. Now stripped before merge; `PGPASSFILE`/`PGSERVICEFILE` nulled to defeat libpq fallback resolution.
- **`_env_truthy("PHASE12_FORCE_CUTOVER=ture")` typo fail-loud (pr-test-analyzer).** Typos in force/confirm env vars now raise SystemExit rather than silently defaulting.

### Removed
- `analytics-service/scripts/phase12_kill_switch.py` — dead `_PG_DSN_ARGV_MARKERS` constant (was unused after the argv → env-var pivot) and unused `audit_line` parameter on `_atomic_append_todos` (comment-analyzer round).

## [0.22.40.0] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — SQL migrations CRITICAL slice + /simplify A+B+C round.** Six new Supabase migrations close the SQL-actionable Critical (C-0284) and High audit findings (H-08xx + H-09xx) on `compute_jobs`, `sanitize_user`, retention crons, `scoring_weight_overrides`, and `commit_scenario_batch`, plus a one-off `_assert_no_public_execute(text)` helper that replaces the brittle `has_function_privilege('public', ...)` probe with a reliable `aclexplode(grantee=0)` ACL inspection. After the initial commits a full `/simplify` pipeline (base reuse/quality/efficiency + comment-analyzer → silent-failure-hunter / code-reviewer / pr-test-analyzer / security specialist fan-out → fresh-context Claude red team) caught **one CRITICAL bug** and several HIGH/MEDIUM issues that would have shipped silent gaps. **The red team caught a wrong weight whitelist** in `_scoring_weight_overrides_is_valid` — the helper rejected the four real keys the engine emits (`W_PORTFOLIO_FIT, W_PREFERENCE_FIT, W_TRACK_RECORD, W_CAPACITY_FIT` per `feedback_engine.py:60-63 / match_engine.py:773-795`) and accepted seven imaginary keys instead. Had this shipped, every existing `allocator_preferences.scoring_weight_overrides` row would have been silently nulled by the backfill and the feedback-engine's next `_persist_overrides` UPDATE would have raised `check_violation` permanently. The Phase A pass also caught **a second CRITICAL** that pre-existed the /simplify work: `sanitize_user`'s sole-admin loop referenced `om1.org_id` (non-existent column — the schema names it `organization_id`) which raised 42703 at runtime, and the broad `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS` trap above it silently swallowed the failure. Every GDPR sanitize that should have flagged an orphan organization emitted zero `organization.orphaned_by_sanitize` audit rows, and the verifier substring probe passed because the action string was still present in the body. Both CRITICALs are fixed; behavioral regression probes were added to the migrations' self-verify DO blocks (column-existence preflight + body substring assertions) so a future copy-edit reintroducing either bug aborts the migration at apply time. Other notable fixes: PG-incompatible subquery-in-CHECK (`allocator_preferences_scoring_weight_overrides_shape` was unloadable as-written, empirically reproduced on PG16 with `cannot use subquery in check constraint` — extracted to an IMMUTABLE helper the CHECK calls); `pg_temp` removed from `commit_scenario_batch`'s search_path so a less-trusted role with `pg_temp` write cannot hijack function resolution inside the SECURITY DEFINER body (the rollback also keeps `pg_catalog` per the C-0284 "restoring a known-bad ACL state is not a rollback" policy); the 50-diff cap inside `commit_scenario_batch` now runs AFTER the mig 131 idempotency reservation so a retry of the same key with a malformed body returns `idempotency_body_mismatch` instead of being intercepted by a 22023 cap error; `pg_column_size` (STABLE, TOAST-aware, PG-version-drift-sensitive) replaced with `octet_length(metadata::text)` (IMMUTABLE, deterministic) on the `compute_jobs.metadata` 8KB cap; the metadata coercion path is now fail-loud (a 1+ MB blob in production is the audit finding to investigate, not silently destroy); `claimed_by` live-row violations RAISE EXCEPTION instead of nulling the column (a healthy worker would otherwise be re-flagged unclaimed and reclaimed by the watchdog); over-broad `WHEN OTHERS` traps on three audit emissions (sanitize x2, commit_scenario_batch x1) narrowed to specific SQLSTATEs so schema-drift errors propagate; `_assert_no_public_execute` wired into the self-verify DO blocks of the four SECURITY DEFINER replacements (`enqueue_compute_job`, `mark_compute_job_done`, `sanitize_user`, `commit_scenario_batch`) and REVOKEd from `service_role` to match the migration-utility-only contract; substring body probes strengthened with `regexp_replace(v_body, '--[^\n]*', '', 'g')` so a commented-out `PERFORM log_audit_event_service` no longer satisfies the regex; a Phase B `set_config('quantalyze.sanitize_user.audit_emit_failed', 'true', true)` GUC observability hook was reverted in Phase C after the red team proved it was dead code (PostgREST's admin.rpc() runs in its own implicit transaction; `is_local=true` GUCs reset at COMMIT, so the caller never sees the value). Net: 20 commits, 1 CRITICAL bug caught at the validation gate, all changes empirically tested on local PG16, both forward + rollback migrations updated.

### Added
- `supabase/migrations/20260515205431_sec_def_public_execute_guard.sql` — installs `public._assert_no_public_execute(text)` helper. Reads `pg_proc.proacl` via `aclexplode(grantee=0)` to assert PUBLIC has no EXECUTE grant on a target function. Replaces `has_function_privilege('public', ...)` which is unreliable for the pseudo-grantee (the legacy probe returns FALSE even when PUBLIC has EXECUTE — exactly the leak C-0284 was opened on). Migration-utility only — REVOKEd from PUBLIC/anon/authenticated/service_role; only `postgres` (superuser) bypasses for migration apply.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210000_compute_jobs_high_hardening.sql` — `compute_jobs_metadata_size_bounded` CHECK (8 KB cap on `octet_length(metadata::text)`), `compute_jobs_claimed_by_safe` CHECK (≤128 chars, charset `[A-Za-z0-9_:./-]+` permitting Railway + Kubernetes worker IDs), partial GIN on `parent_job_ids` restricted to live statuses (`pending`/`running`/`done_pending_children`), and `mark_compute_job_done` body rewrite using `parent_job_ids @> ARRAY[p_job_id]::uuid[]` containment operator + GET DIAGNOSTICS to surface 0-row child UPDATEs.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql` — `pg_advisory_xact_lock` on the GDPR sanitize path serializes concurrent admin invocations; sole-admin organization detection emits `organization.orphaned_by_sanitize` audit rows BEFORE the `organization_members` DELETE so the operator follow-up trail (transfer ownership / archive / hard-delete with consent) is preserved; the sanitize itself emits one `gdpr.sanitize_user` audit row per successful run. Drops the legacy `idx_deletion_requests_pending` index in favor of the v2 partial-index (`pending` status only).
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210200_retention_crons_high_hardening.sql` — composite index on `notification_dispatches(notification_type, recipient_email, created_at DESC)` for the api_key_rotation_reminder NOT EXISTS subquery; BTREE on `audit_log(created_at)` to serve the nightly hot→cold cron range scan. Reschedules `audit_log_hot_to_cold` (emits NOTICE on ON CONFLICT UUID collision), `retention_notification_dispatches` (preserves `status='queued'` rows + new NOTICE when >1000 aged-queued rows accumulate so an un-drained consumer surface), and `retention_compute_jobs_failed` (uses `COALESCE(next_attempt_at, created_at)` cutoff so a `failed_retry` row in slow-burn recovery isn't reaped mid-recovery).
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210300_scoring_weight_overrides_high_hardening.sql` — installs `public._scoring_weight_overrides_is_valid(jsonb)` IMMUTABLE helper called from both the backfill predicate AND the `allocator_preferences_scoring_weight_overrides_shape` CHECK constraint (single source of truth, legal use inside CHECK). Whitelist matches `feedback_engine.py:60-63 ALL_DIMENSIONS` exactly: `{W_PORTFOLIO_FIT, W_PREFERENCE_FIT, W_TRACK_RECORD, W_CAPACITY_FIT}` with values in `[0.5, 1.5]` (engine's `_clamp` range). Adds defense-in-depth `auth.uid() = p_allocator_id OR auth.role() = 'service_role'` gate to `enqueue_compute_job`'s allocator branch with both `auth.role()` and `auth.uid()` captured in one BEGIN/EXCEPTION block (narrowed to undefined_function / undefined_table / insufficient_privilege).
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — composite index `allocator_holdings_ownership_probe_idx (allocator_id, venue, symbol, holding_type, asof DESC)` for the per-diff ownership probe. New `scenario.commit` audit_log row per successful batch attributed to the allocator (fail-soft with narrow EXCEPTION trap). The 50-diff cap is enforced inside the RPC mirroring the route-layer zod cap so a direct `supabase.rpc('commit_scenario_batch', ...)` can't DoS the function via 100k-element arrays.
- `supabase/migrations/down/*-rollback.sql` (6 files) — rollback for every forward migration. `commit_scenario_batch` rollback keeps `search_path = public, pg_catalog` (not `pg_temp`) per the policy that restoring a known-bad ACL/posture is not a rollback.

### Changed
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210300_scoring_weight_overrides_high_hardening.sql` — Phase C CRITICAL fix: the H-0939 whitelist embedded SEVEN imaginary weight slot names (`W_PORTFOLIO_FIT, W_PERFORMANCE, W_QUALITY, W_RISK_FIT, W_RELIABILITY, W_EXCHANGE_FIT, W_LIQUIDITY_FIT`). The actual engine uses FOUR weights — only `W_PORTFOLIO_FIT` overlapped. Had this shipped, every existing `allocator_preferences.scoring_weight_overrides` row containing `W_PREFERENCE_FIT`/`W_TRACK_RECORD`/`W_CAPACITY_FIT` (the real keys) would have been silently nulled by the backfill, and `feedback_engine._persist_overrides` (analytics-service/services/feedback_engine.py:238) would have raised `check_violation` on every UPDATE permanently. Fix verified empirically on PG16 against the 7 realistic shapes in `test_feedback_engine.py`.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210300_scoring_weight_overrides_high_hardening.sql` — Phase A CRITICAL fix: the original CHECK constraint embedded `NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM jsonb_object_keys(...))` subqueries inline. PostgreSQL forbids subqueries in CHECK expressions and raises `0A000 cannot_use_subquery_in_check`, aborting the entire migration before STEP 2-4 can run (empirically reproduced on PG16). Extracted the predicate into the IMMUTABLE `_scoring_weight_overrides_is_valid` helper so the CHECK can call it legally and the backfill predicate shares one source of truth.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql` — Phase A CRITICAL fix: the sole-admin orphan-detection loop referenced `om1.org_id` and `om2.org_id` — non-existent columns. The schema (`20260405180928_organizations.sql:12`) names the column `organization_id`. At runtime the loop raised 42703 `column "org_id" does not exist` which the broad `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN RAISE NOTICE` trap silently swallowed. Every GDPR sanitize call that should have flagged an orphan organization actually emitted ZERO orphan audit_log rows. Renamed to `organization_id`, widened the role filter from `'admin'` to `IN ('owner','admin')` to match the schema's CHECK, added a pre-flight `information_schema.columns` probe in the self-verify DO block so schema drift surfaces at apply time, and added regression substring probes (`om1.organization_id` + `role IN ('owner', 'admin')`) so a future copy-edit reintroducing the typo aborts the migration.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — `search_path` locked to `(public, pg_catalog)` (was `public, pg_temp`). `pg_temp` is the search-path hijack vector that the rest of the audit-slice SECURITY DEFINER functions deliberately exclude — a less-trusted role with `pg_temp` write could pre-create a same-name function/operator and hijack resolution inside the privileged body. The rollback also keeps `pg_catalog` per policy.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — 50-diff cap reordered to run AFTER the mig 131 idempotency reservation. A retry of the same Idempotency-Key with a malformed body (e.g., 51 diffs) was previously masked by a 22023 cap error and never reached the `idempotency_body_mismatch` hash check. Now retries are served from the idempotency cache (or get the body-mismatch signal); first-ever oversized calls atomically roll back the `in_flight` reservation since the cap raises before any mutating work runs.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210000_compute_jobs_high_hardening.sql` — `pg_column_size(metadata)` (STABLE, TOAST-aware, version-drift-sensitive, emits a CHECK-creation WARNING) replaced with `octet_length(metadata::text)` (IMMUTABLE, deterministic, matches the 32 KB ceiling shape `log_audit_event_service` already uses). Metadata coercion path now fail-loud: a 1+ MB blob is the audit finding to investigate, not silently destroy. `claimed_by` regex widened to permit `/` (Kubernetes-style worker IDs); live-row violations RAISE EXCEPTION (not silent coerce) so the watchdog can't reclaim a healthy worker that wrote a non-conforming identifier; terminal-state coercions emit per-row NOTICE with the original value so the migration apply log carries the forensic trail.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql` — narrow `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS` traps on the orphan-emission and gdpr-emission paths replaced with `WHEN unique_violation OR check_violation OR string_data_right_truncation OR numeric_value_out_of_range OR insufficient_privilege` so schema-drift errors (42703 / 42P01 / 42883) propagate instead of being swallowed. Same narrowing applied to `commit_scenario_batch`'s `scenario.commit` audit emission.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210300_scoring_weight_overrides_high_hardening.sql` — `auth.role()` capture in the allocator-branch ownership gate now also captures `auth.uid()` once into `v_caller_uid` so the function isn't invoked twice. EXCEPTION trap narrowed to `undefined_function OR undefined_table OR insufficient_privilege` so schema drift propagates instead of silently downgrading both vars to NULL.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210200_retention_crons_high_hardening.sql` — `retention_notification_dispatches` cron body wrapped in a `DO $body$ ... END $body$` block that also emits `RAISE NOTICE` when >1000 queued rows aged >180d accumulate. The H-0910 fix correctly preserves queued reminders so an un-consumed signal isn't silently purged, but absent the Sprint-7 consumer the new NOTICE is the only operator signal that the queue is filling up.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210000_compute_jobs_high_hardening.sql`, `20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql`, `20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — each SECURITY DEFINER self-verify DO block now PERFORM-s `public._assert_no_public_execute(...)` on its own function signature (4 sites total). The REVOKE strips any leak; the helper raises `insufficient_privilege` if a future migration ever re-grants PUBLIC.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515205431_sec_def_public_execute_guard.sql` — `_assert_no_public_execute` REVOKE extended to `service_role` alongside `PUBLIC, anon, authenticated`. The function comment promises "migration-utility only" and migrations run as `postgres` (superuser) which bypasses any per-role REVOKE; a compromised service-role token has no legitimate need for this helper.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — removed redundant `DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.commit_scenario_batch(uuid, jsonb, text, text)` before `CREATE OR REPLACE`. The DROP was cargo-culted from mig 131 (which legitimately changed signature 2-arg → 4-arg). Removed both from the forward migration and the rollback for symmetry — `CREATE OR REPLACE` alone preserves ACLs and dependent objects atomically with no "function not found" window for concurrent rpc() callers.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql`, `20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — substring body probes now strip SQL `--` comments via `regexp_replace(v_body, '--[^\n]*', '', 'g')` before regex matching. `pg_get_functiondef` preserves comments verbatim, so a commented-out `PERFORM log_audit_event_service` would otherwise satisfy the original `~* 'PERFORM\s+public\.log_audit_event_service[^;]*'` probe and pass verification while the call site was dead.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210200_retention_crons_high_hardening.sql` — comment fix: header bullet at line 64 and JOB 3 inline comment at line 199 no longer name a non-existent `'error'` status. The `notification_dispatches.status` CHECK enforces `IN ('queued', 'sent', 'failed')` — there is no `'error'` value, and a future maintainer copying the comment into a CHECK update or TypeScript union would have invented one.
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210400_commit_scenario_batch_high_hardening.sql` — header bullet at line 8 now reads "three clusters of SQL-actionable HIGH defects (four individual H-IDs)" matching the H-0974/H-0976/H-0977/H-0984 count. Fail-soft justification at line ~75 rewritten so the policy reads as "missing audit is recoverable; rolled-back commit silently discards the user-visible action" rather than the inverted-feeling original.

### Fixed
- **CRITICAL: `_scoring_weight_overrides_is_valid` whitelist mismatch with the engine.** See `## Changed` for the full root-cause analysis. Verification: 7 realistic engine shapes from `test_feedback_engine.py` (`{"W_PREFERENCE_FIT":0.5}`, `{"W_PORTFOLIO_FIT":1.5}`, `{"W_TRACK_RECORD":1.0}`, `{"W_CAPACITY_FIT":0.5}`, plus 3 multi-key combinations and NULL) all accepted by the corrected CHECK; previous stale keys (`W_QUALITY`, `W_PERFORMANCE`, `W_RISK_FIT`) all rejected.
- **CRITICAL: `sanitize_user` sole-admin loop column reference + silently-swallowed runtime error.** See `## Changed`. Regression armor: information_schema column-existence preflight in STEP 3 + body substring probes for `om1.organization_id` and `role IN ('owner', 'admin')` so a future copy-edit reintroducing the typo aborts the migration apply.
- **CRITICAL: `allocator_preferences_scoring_weight_overrides_shape` CHECK with inline subqueries.** PostgreSQL forbids subqueries in CHECK expressions; the migration would have aborted with `0A000 cannot use subquery in check constraint` before STEP 2-4 could run. Extracted to IMMUTABLE helper that the CHECK calls — single source of truth shared with the backfill predicate. Empirically reproduced + fix verified on PG16.
- `commit_scenario_batch` rollback was silently restoring `search_path = public, pg_temp` — undoing the Phase A `pg_catalog` hardening. Now keeps `pg_catalog` per the C-0284 "restoring a known-bad posture is not a rollback" policy.

### Documented
- `supabase/migrations/20260515210100_sanitize_user_high_hardening.sql` — function COMMENT documents the partial-success observability gap. When a fail-soft `EXCEPTION` swallows an `audit_log` emission failure the route handler cannot detect it from the BOOLEAN return contract. Closing this gap requires either a forensic side-table the route can SELECT or a richer JSONB return contract — deferred to a follow-up rather than landed as an invasive change in this audit slice.

## [0.22.38.1] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — finalize-wizard hardening + /simplify A+B+C round.** Ten audit findings closed on `/api/strategies/finalize-wizard` (C-0119, H-0322 through H-0331) followed by a full /simplify pipeline (base reviewers → 4-specialist fan-out → red-team adversarial). Wizard submissions can no longer (a) be probed by an attacker who guesses a victim's strategy_id, (b) silently corrupt founder-notification emails when the RPC sanitizes the name, (c) leave the strategy stuck at `computation_status='pending'` after Round-2 cutover, (d) coerce an invalid AUM/max_capacity to NULL on a "Verified by Quantalyze" factsheet, (e) leak `INTERNAL_API_TOKEN` into Vercel runtime logs via fetch/retry wrapper error stringification (the safeName/safeMessage extraction missed wrapped errors whose .name or .message embed the token), or (f) silently respond 200 with an empty body when `/process-key` returns an unrecognized envelope shape. The discriminated union on `ProcessKeyOnboardResponse` also rejects mixed `{queued: true, code: "WIZARD_DUPLICATE"}` envelopes that would otherwise double-process a strategy. A load-bearing comment + audit pragma flag the unified-path side-effect parity gap (founder email + last_sync_at touch + sync_trades enqueue are absent from the Python `/process-key` worker) so the eventual `isUnifiedBackboneActive=true` cutover surfaces the architectural decision rather than silently dropping three founder-visible side effects.

### Added
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — belt-and-braces `.eq('user_id', user.id)` ownership filter on the strategies SELECT (C-0119/H-0329). Defense-in-depth on RLS: if RLS regresses, an attacker who guesses a victim's strategy_id can no longer (a) trigger the Railway scope-broadening probe revealing it's a real API-keyed strategy, then (b) read `api_keys.exchange` through the admin client.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — fail-LOUD validation on `aum` and `max_capacity` (H-0325/H-0326). Invalid values (negative, NaN, `1e500` → Infinity, non-number, ≥1e12 ceiling) now return 400 with a typed error instead of silently coercing to NULL and finalizing a Verified-tier factsheet with missing AUM.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — `enqueue_compute_job` for `sync_trades` in the `after()` block (H-0330), gated by `USE_COMPUTE_JOBS_QUEUE`. Pre-fix the only enqueue lived in `/api/keys/sync` behind a manual "Sync now" button; removing that button on cutover would orphan every new wizard submission at `computation_status='pending'`. `cron/reconcile-strategies` is the 24h backstop.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — module-scope `scrubInternalToken` + `safeErrorString` helpers (H-0328 / Phase C simplify). The original H-0328 fix only stripped raw error objects from the probe-error log; it missed wrapped/retry errors whose `.name` or `.message` embed the literal `X-Internal-Token: $INTERNAL_API_TOKEN`. Active substring replacement now covers every error-formatting site (probe error, `keyLinkErr.message`, `keyRowErr.message`, after()-block side-effect rejection `r.reason`).
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — `isProcessKeyOnboardResponse` discriminated union type guard (H-0327 / Phase B+C simplify). Original guard accepted any object with a `queued` key; tightened to require typed fields, then split into `{queued: true, verification_id: string} | {queued: false, code: string, …}`. Mixed envelopes (`{queued: true, code: "WIZARD_DUPLICATE"}`) now route to a 502 + Sentry contract-violation path instead of double-processing.
- 12 regression tests for the previously untested audit paths: H-0322 `keyLinkErr` Sentry escalation; H-0323 `keyRowErr` Sentry escalation; H-0327 fall-through (string `queued`, missing `queued`, mixed envelope, missing `verification_id` on `queued=true`, missing `code` on `queued=false`); H-0328 `INTERNAL_API_TOKEN` scrub on wrapped errors with token in `.name`/`.message`; H-0330 `enqueue_compute_job` failure → Promise.allSettled rejection → Sentry escalation; WIZARD_DUPLICATE valid envelope translation; C-0119/H-0329 `.eq('user_id', ...)` filter assertion.

### Changed
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — captures and Sentry-escalates the `api_key_id` lookup error in the `after()` block via the existing `@/lib/sentry-capture` helper (H-0322 + Phase A simplify dedup). Pre-fix a transient PG blip silently dropped the `api_keys.last_sync_at` touch, which Sprint-2 GC would then treat as an abandoned key and reclaim.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — captures and Sentry-escalates the unified-path `api_keys.exchange` lookup error (H-0323 + Phase B-1). Pre-fix a `console.warn` line was best-effort log capture, not alertable; a transient PG blip could silently route a Binance/Bybit key through the OKX-specific code path. Now mirrors the H-0322 escalation pattern.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — surfaces `/process-key` contract violations as 502 + Sentry instead of `NextResponse.json(upstream ?? {})` with 200 (Phase B-2). Pre-fix the wizard would silently render "success" with no strategy_id when the upstream envelope shape drifted.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — drops redundant `as string` casts after `isUuid` (H-0324). `isUuid` is a TypeScript type predicate; the casts were dead weight at the parse boundary.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — founder-notify email name (`notifyFounderNewStrategy`) reads from the DB row `strategies.name` after the SECURITY DEFINER RPC instead of the form input (H-0331). Pre-fix `fields.name` would diverge from the admin UI any time the RPC sanitized/transformed the name; now both surfaces share one source of truth.
