- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — replaced 2 ad-hoc `try { await import("@sentry/nextjs"); Sentry.captureException(...) }` blocks with the existing `captureToSentry` helper (Phase A simplify). Eliminates ~25 lines of duplication and aligns with the 4 other call sites the helper was created to consolidate.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — tightened `isProcessKeyOnboardResponse` to a discriminated union on `queued` (Phase B-3 + C-2). `queued: true` branch now requires `verification_id: string` and forbids `code`/`idempotent` (mixed envelope = bug); `queued: false` branch requires `code: string`. The previous `?? true` fallback that invented a `queued: true` from `{queued: undefined}` is gone.
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.test.ts` — hoisted `mockProbeReadOnly` (3+ in-file duplicates → 1 module-scope helper) and added deterministic `flushAfter()` that awaits the tracked after-callback promise instead of guessing microtask ticks (Phase A + B-10).
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — comment cleanup: dropped duplicate `@audit-skip` block, removed `M-18: extracted from POST()` refactor narration, collapsed triple-restated H-0331 explanation (Phase A simplify per project no-comments-explaining-WHAT rule).

### Fixed
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — H-0328 defense-in-depth: the original `safeName`/`safeMessage` extraction assumed `INTERNAL_API_TOKEN` never appears in `Error.name` or `Error.message` directly. Wrapped retry errors (which stringify the outgoing init into the wrapper message) can land the live token there. Fix: active substring scrub on every error-formatting site, promoted to a module-scope helper after the new regression test exposed the gap.

### Documented
- `src/app/api/strategies/finalize-wizard/route.ts` — load-bearing comment block on `unifiedFinalizeWizardHandler` flags the side-effect parity gap (`notify_founder_new_strategy`, `api_keys_last_sync_at_touch`, `enqueue_sync_trades_job` all run in the legacy `after()` but the Python `/process-key` worker only enqueues `process_key_long`, so flipping `isUnifiedBackboneActive=true` silently drops three founder-visible side effects). Architectural decision (where do these belong?) is out of scope for /simplify; the comment surfaces the gap so the cutover team can place them appropriately.

## [0.22.39.0] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — cron.py /simplify A+B+C round.** Closes the audit hardening series on the cron-sync endpoint with the same 3-phase pipeline used on the prior rounds: base simplify + comment-analyzer, 4-specialist fan-out (silent-failure-hunter, type-design-analyzer, pr-test-analyzer, code-reviewer), then a fresh-context Claude red team. Net effect for the operator: a daily cron tick that fails any of its sub-steps is now visible in the response counters and the cron runner's alarms instead of disappearing into 200-OK silence. A wedged portfolio recompute no longer holds the shared `_compute_semaphore(3)` slot forever — it times out at 90s; cron also caps its own concurrency at 2 so a backlog tick can't starve live `POST /api/portfolio-analytics` traffic for hours. An in-flight `computation_status='computing'` row is now its own response bucket (`in_flight`, not silently bucketed as `ok`) so a stuck row can't masquerade as a successful tick on the dashboard. One per-strategy `sync_trades` RPC failing inside a multi-strategy key no longer aborts the whole key — the other strategies still land, `last_sync_at` still bumps (no trade re-fetch), and the result reports `status: "partial"` with `strategy_errors[sid]`. A transient validation failure (rate-limit, network, exchange-down) shows up in the new `transient` counter instead of being silently uncounted. An initial `api_keys` SELECT exception now raises a typed HTTP 500 with `logger.exception` (was a raw FastAPI traceback). A recompute lookup blip preserves per-key sync results in the response payload (with `lookup_error`) instead of losing the entire `results` array. The `failures` list is capped at 50 entries with `failures_truncated` / `total_failures` so a platform-wide Supabase outage can't bloat the response body in Sentry / log aggregators.

### Added
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — module constants `PORTFOLIO_RECOMPUTE_TIMEOUT` (90s), `CRON_RECOMPUTE_CONCURRENCY` (2), `RECOMPUTE_FAILURE_CAP` (50). The why-it-matters (semaphore starvation, response body bloat, wedge bounds) lives at the constant definition site once instead of being scattered across inline comments at the use sites.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `SyncStatus` / `RecomputeStatus` Literal aliases documenting the wire-format status set. Internal assignments annotate the type so a typo (`"OK"`, `"errored"`) is caught at type-check time before it silently misclassifies a result in the summary counters.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — new `in_flight` recompute bucket. When `portfolio_analytics` already has a `computation_status='computing'` row for a portfolio, `_guarded_recompute` returns `(pid, "in_flight", None)` instead of `(pid, "ok", None)`. An alert built on `pr["failed"] == 0 and pr["ok"] == pr["attempted"]` no longer silently treats a stuck "computing" row (the other worker may have died) as a successful tick.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `partial` per-key status. A multi-strategy key where some `sync_trades` RPCs succeed and others fail now reports `status: "partial"` with `strategy_errors[sid]: "ExceptionType: message"` and `per_strategy_stored[sid]: 0` for the failures. The remaining strategies' stored counts are preserved. If EVERY RPC fails, the key flips to `status: "error"` (not `"partial"`) so an operator isn't misled into thinking trades were stored when none were.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `transient` summary counter + response key. Transient validation failures (RATE_LIMITED / NETWORK_UNAVAILABLE / DDOS_PROTECTION / EXCHANGE_UNAVAILABLE / VALIDATION_UNEXPECTED) were previously counted by NONE of the summary buckets — a tick where every key hit a transient failure logged "0 synced, 0 failed = idle" and the response payload mirrored that. Now visible in both the structured log line and the response body.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `failures_truncated` / `total_failures` keys on `portfolio_recomputes`. When the per-portfolio failure count exceeds `RECOMPUTE_FAILURE_CAP`, the returned `failures` list is capped at the cap and the truncation is surfaced so the operator sees both the bounded list and the true count.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `portfolio_recomputes.lookup_error`. A Supabase blip on the `portfolio_strategies` or `portfolios` SELECT now lands in this field instead of letting the exception propagate and lose the per-key `results` array.
- 14 regression tests in `analytics-service/tests/test_cron_router.py` covering: deactivate UPDATE no-op detection, partial status with strategy_errors, sync_trades shape-fallback ERROR log, api_keys SELECT failure → typed 500 + logger.exception, in-flight `computing` skip as its own bucket, `validate_key_permissions` raising → status="error" with no deactivation, transient + partial counter surfaces in summary + response, HTTPException(400) → `skipped` (not `failed`), per-portfolio `asyncio.wait_for` timeout → `failed` with `TimeoutError` repr, `failures` list truncation with truncated/total counts, recompute lookup error preserves sync results, all-RPCs-failing yields `error` (not `partial`), partial key cascades only successful strategies into recompute, cron-internal concurrency cap leaves a shared-semaphore slot for live HTTP traffic.

### Changed
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — initial `api_keys` SELECT wrapped in try/except. Pre-fix any Supabase exception surfaced as an unhandled 500 with a raw FastAPI traceback in the body; the cron alarm fired but on-call had nothing actionable. Now caught and re-raised as `HTTPException(500, detail="cron_sync: api_keys SELECT failed: <ExceptionType>")` with a structured `logger.exception` entry.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_sync_single_key`'s per-strategy `sync_trades` loop now wraps each RPC individually. Pre-fix one failing RPC aborted the whole fan-out, the surviving strategies' trades were lost, `last_sync_at` did NOT bump (so the next tick refetched the same trades), and the result was marked `status="error"` even if other strategies had stored successfully. Post-fix each RPC has its own try/except; failures land in `strategy_errors[sid]`, successes are preserved in `per_strategy_stored`, and `last_sync_at` always bumps so we don't lose the cursor on the strategies that did land.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `sync_trades` non-int return shape now logs an ERROR with the unexpected payload before falling back to `len(trades)`. The fallback is necessary (the function can't crash on a Postgres function signature change mid-flight) but it now surfaces the contract drift so the next operator review catches it.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — credential-rejection deactivate UPDATE now inspects `.data` after `.execute()`. An empty `.data` (the row was deleted or re-keyed between the cron SELECT and the UPDATE) now logs an ERROR; pre-fix the no-op looked identical to a successful deactivation in the logs.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_sync_key_with_timeout` now logs an exchange-leak warning on `asyncio.TimeoutError` so the symptom ("Unclosed connector" warnings spamming logs) maps back to a specific key id instead of being a mystery.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_guarded_recompute` wraps `_compute_portfolio_analytics(pid)` in `asyncio.wait_for(PORTFOLIO_RECOMPUTE_TIMEOUT=90)`. A wedged compute on one portfolio no longer holds the shared `_compute_semaphore(3)` slot indefinitely (and starve every live `POST /api/portfolio-analytics` request for hours).
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_guarded_recompute` now classifies `HTTPException(400)` ("No strategies", "No returns data") as `skipped`, not `failed`. Pre-fix benign business states opened a Sentry ticket per portfolio per tick.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — recompute lookup (`portfolio_strategies` + `portfolios` SELECTs) wrapped in try/except. A Supabase blip on either lookup now lands in `portfolio_recomputes.lookup_error` and the response payload still carries the per-key `results` array; pre-fix the exception escaped and the whole sync result set was lost.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `cron_recompute_sem = asyncio.Semaphore(CRON_RECOMPUTE_CONCURRENCY)` added INSIDE `cron_sync`. Cron's own gather is now bounded so a 500-portfolio backlog tick can't claim every shared-semaphore slot for hours and starve live HTTP requests.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `synced_strategy_ids` cascade now includes `partial` keys, iterating `per_strategy_stored` (instead of `strategy_ids`) and filtering to entries where `stored > 0`. A partial key's successful secondaries now trigger portfolio recompute; pre-fix only `status="ok"` keys cascaded, so partial keys' downstream portfolios silently missed their recompute window.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_compute_semaphore` comment downgraded from misleading "DB-level check fixes same-pod race" prose to an honest "Semaphore(3) admits 3 concurrent coroutines, the DB-level in-flight check is itself TOCTOU between SELECT and the implicit INSERT" statement. A real cross-process guard would need a `UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE computation_status='computing'` migration; tracked separately.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — module-level constants `CREDENTIAL_REJECTION_CODES` and `ALLOWED_STRATEGY_STATUSES` (previously inline in their consuming branches). Same pattern the rest of the file uses for `KEY_SYNC_TIMEOUT` / `BATCH_SIZE` — a future reader extending the taxonomy can find the authoritative set in one place.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — audit-archaeology comment trim (`audit-2026-05-07 C/H-0XXX` tags + "pre-fix did X..." narration removed from production comments per the project's no-task-reference-rot rule). Git history is the authoritative source; production comments now state the invariant only. Test-file comments naming the audit finding stay (explicit project convention for regression-test traceability).
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — outer `except Exception` in `_sync_single_key` switched from `logger.error(..., exc_info=True)` to `logger.exception(...)` to match the standard the file's own comment (`logger.exception (not logger.error(str(e))) so the traceback reaches Sentry`) was promising at the recompute site but not delivering at the per-key site.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — `_sync_key_with_timeout` `strategy_id` derivation reuses `strategy_ids[0]` instead of the dead `key_row.get("strategy_id")` fallback. The fallback would only fire if `strategy_ids` was empty AND a future caller populated `strategy_id` independently — exactly the singular-vs-plural drift that C-0201 was opened to kill.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_cron_router.py` — `TestRevokedKeyBranch.test_credential_rejection_deactivates_key_scoped_to_id` now asserts the UPDATE payload is exactly `{"is_active": False}` (no other fields). A regression that flipped to `{"is_active": True}` or added `{"user_id": None}` would otherwise still pass the prior `.eq("id", key_id)`-scoped assertion.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_cron_router.py` — `test_status_filter_drops_archived_and_unknown` replaced with `test_only_live_statuses_receive_sync_trades_rpc` that actually drives `cron_sync` end-to-end and asserts on the resulting `sync_trades` RPC fan-out. The prior test re-implemented the filter inline (a refactor that dropped the cron.py filter would have left that test green); the prior `test_cron_source_pulls_status_from_embed` static source-pin (brittle to reformat) is removed.

### Fixed
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — H-0541 / H-0545 closure on cron-sync's portfolio recompute now reports per-portfolio outcomes with a status discriminator so a 100%-failure tick is no longer indistinguishable from a healthy 200 OK. Pre-fix the response payload showed `failed=0` even when every recompute raised because the broad `except` swallowed exceptions before the counters incremented.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — C-0192 / C-0194 hardening on the credential vs transient validation branch. The deactivate path now lights up only for the four credential-rejection codes (`AUTH_FAILED`, `PERMISSION_DENIED`, `WITHDRAW_SCOPE`, `TRADE_SCOPE`); transient codes (rate-limit / network / DDoS / exchange-down / unexpected) leave the key active so a 30s network blip can't permanently disable a user's key.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — C-0199 closure on the KEK-missing branch. `cron_sync` now raises `HTTPException(500)` instead of returning HTTP 200 with `{"error": "Encryption not configured"}` in the body. The cron runner only alarms on non-2xx; a 200 + error-in-body kept KEK outages silent for days.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — C-0200 closure on the strategy lifecycle filter. The cron now joins only strategies whose status is one of `{draft, pending_review, published}`; archived/suspended/deleted strategies are dropped. Pre-fix the cron joined every linked strategy regardless of status and could overwrite the submission snapshot of an archived strategy, flipping its approval-gate verdict. Missing-`status` strategies now fail closed (dropped) instead of fail-open.
- `analytics-service/routers/cron.py` — C-0201 closure on multi-strategy keys. A single api_keys row can back N strategies (`strategies.api_key_id` FK); pre-fix the loop took `strategy_rel[0]` only and silently missed strategies 2..N every cron tick. Post-fix `sync_trades` is invoked once per linked strategy and `last_sync_at` is updated once.

## [0.22.38.0] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — exchange.py / match_eval.py / analytics_runner.py /simplify A+B+C round.** Continues the audit hardening with three more silent-failure closures plus a /simplify pipeline (base reviewers → 6-specialist fan-out → red-team adversarial). Allocators on Binance with >1000 fills or snapshots stop silently dropping symbols at PostgREST's 1000-row ceiling (the H-0662 pagination fix was being defeated upstream at the symbol-discovery step). A Binance per-symbol fan-out where every symbol 500's no longer cements a false-success 0-fills sync — it now raises a typed `BinancePerSymbolFetchError` so the worker marks the job failed_retry. The match-engine eval dashboard now surfaces a `skipped` / `skipped_rate` so a run where 80% of intros raise during processing is distinguishable from a healthy 20-intro run. CCXT fills with `timestamp=0` (epoch) or `timestamp=True` (bool, int subclass) no longer produce 1970-01-01 phantom rows that corrupt FIFO position reconstruction. OKX/Bybit ts-drop log lines stop dumping the raw fill body (defends against log injection + PII leak in aggregator pipelines).

### Added
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — `BinancePerSymbolFetchError` typed exception. Raised when ALL per-symbol fetches fail in the fan-out (partial-success contract preserved). Carries `failed_symbols` list + the first underlying error for triage. Mirrors the `ColdStartSymbolDiscoveryError` contract: a 100%-failure run is a structural fault, not silent zero-fills.
- `analytics-service/services/match_eval.py` — `compute_hit_rate_metrics` returns `skipped` and `skipped_rate` keys in BOTH the success path and the early-empty path. Pre-fix `skipped` was a local counter that shrank the denominator silently; an 80% bad-row rate looked identical to a healthy run with a smaller denominator.
- `analytics-service/services/db.py` — `paginated_select` raises explicit `TypeError` on `str` `order_by` input with a helpful migration message. Pre-fix a stray str would unpack character-by-character through the `for column, desc` loop and surface a cryptic `ValueError: not enough values to unpack`.
- 11 regression tests in `analytics-service/tests/`: `_normalize_fill` drops epoch (`timestamp=0`), bool (`timestamp=True`), missing/non-numeric price/amount; logs WARN on primary-field parse failure before falling back; OKX cursor walks `data[-1]` (oldest) across 3 non-empty pages; Binance per-symbol total-failure raises typed exception; partial-failure preserves the partial-success contract + logs a count summary; semaphore-inside-loop concurrency probe (ETH-p1 completes before BTC-p2); `compute_hit_rate_metrics` surfaces `skipped_count`; `win_rate_boundary` 13-case parametrize across the 1.5 threshold + ULP drift + non-finite producer drift.

### Changed
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — `_normalize_fill` delegates to the existing `services.ingestion._timestamps.coerce_to_aware_utc` helper instead of re-implementing 30 lines of timestamp coercion. Return type now `FillRow | None` (the `# type: ignore[return-value]` cast is gone).
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — `_normalize_fill` now drops fills with missing/non-numeric `price` or `amount`. Pre-fix `float(trade.get("price", 0))` silently substituted `0` — same FIFO-poisoning shape as the H-0673 timestamp bug, just one field over.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — `_normalize_fill` timestamp skip filter changed from `raw == 0` to `not raw or isinstance(raw, bool)`. Python's `True` is an `int` subclass; `True == 0` is False, so `timestamp=True` passed the prior check and produced 1970-01-01T00:00:00.001 phantom rows.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — `_normalize_fill` logs WARN per iteration when a primary timestamp field (`timestamp`) parses-fails before falling back to `datetime`. Pre-fix the silent fall-through masked upstream producer drift that poisoned the canonical cursor field.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — Binance `_fetch_raw_trades_binance` `_get_symbols` now uses `paginated_select` for both `trades.symbol` and `position_snapshots.symbol`. The bare 1000-row SELECT silently truncated symbol discovery on strategies with >1000 fill or snapshot rows, defeating the H-0662 pagination fix that paginated per-symbol fetches.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — Binance per-symbol semaphore moved inside the per-page loop. One heavy 20-page symbol no longer monopolizes a `Semaphore(5)` slot for 20 sequential RTTs while 4 sibling symbols block.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — Binance markets normalization hoisted into a `{normalized_symbol: ccxt_symbol}` dict built once per fetch. Was O(N_symbols × M_markets) per sync — at 50 symbols × 1500 Binance markets that's 75K string allocations per `sync_trades` job.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — OKX/Bybit ts-drop log lines redact `fill=%r` to whitelisted fields (`instId`/`tradeId`, `symbol`/`execId`). Pre-fix an exchange (or MITM, or compromised upstream) returning a crafted string with embedded newlines/ANSI/log-prefix tokens could forge log lines in downstream aggregators; the full fill body also included account-correlatable identifiers (`ordId`, `tradeId`) beyond what triage requires.
- `analytics-service/services/db.py` — `_paginated_select` renamed to `paginated_select` (underscore-prefixed cross-module imports were a convention smell flagged across 5 import sites). The `match_eval.py` `__all__` re-export is gone; the dead `str` shape for `order_by` is removed.
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — `_finite_pnl` body reduced to a single delegation to `services.metrics._safe_float(t.get("realized_pnl"))`. The `_compute_derived_trade_metrics` `win_rate` / `avg_winning_trade` / `avg_losing_trade` boundary checks collapse to `_safe_float(...) or 0.0` (drops 3 inline `math.isfinite` guards).
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — triple-`for` list comprehensions in SQN / profit-factor unrolled into a single pass. The walrus-style `for v in [_finite_pnl(t)] if v is not None` idiom was a maintainability hazard.
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` / `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — audit-archaeology comment trim (ticket prefixes + "pre-fix this was..." narration removed across OKX/Bybit/Binance branches and `_compute_derived_trade_metrics`). Git history is the authoritative source; comments now state the invariant only.
- `analytics-service/routers/match.py` — `_load_holding_portfolio_context` docstring drops the line-number reference (`called via asyncio.to_thread at line ~268` — the line was actually at 586 after intervening refactors, demonstrating exactly why line refs in comments rot).

### Fixed
- `analytics-service/services/exchange.py` — Binance per-symbol fan-out total failure no longer silently drops every fill. Pre-fix `asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True)` captured every exception and the function returned an empty fills list — same false-success shape that `ColdStartSymbolDiscoveryError` was added to prevent (G12.B.1), one layer up.
- `analytics-service/services/match_eval.py` — `if not valid_intros` early return now preserves the `skipped` count. Pre-fix a 100%-malformed intros run returned `_empty_metrics(lookback_days)` with `skipped=0` — operationally indistinguishable from a 0-intros run.

## [0.22.37.0] - 2026-05-16

**audit-2026-05-07 — queries.ts / match.py / analytics_runner.py simplify + specialist + red-team round.** Closes 8 audit fixes already on the branch (H-0481/H-0484/H-0488/H-0489/H-0490/H-0494/H-0496/H-0499/H-0502/C-0172) plus a /simplify pipeline that added 6-specialist fan-out and a Claude fresh-context red team. Net effect: defense-in-depth on every hardening commit. Allocators on the dashboard get a fail-loud signal when supabase errors hit the candidate-strategies SELECT (instead of an empty flagged-holdings panel masking real breach signals); operators get a structural-fault signal on cron-recompute total failures (`status: "total_failure"` / `"degraded"` instead of silently green 200s); a 1M-row strategy hitting the pagination hard-cap now surfaces a typed exception with truncation context (page count + hint) through `run_strategy_analytics` instead of getting downgraded to a generic 500 retried forever; bridge_outcome rows missing required fields or carrying invalid enum values now throw at the boundary instead of producing `{ id: undefined, kind: undefined }` objects.

### Added
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `EXPECTED_LAZY_METRICS_KINDS_BY_PANEL` runtime mirror of `LazyMetricsPanelKindMap`. `fetchStrategyLazyMetrics` now drops unexpected keys (catches a SQL CASE typo like `rollig_sortino_3m` that previously sailed through the `as` cast) and escalates to Sentry with `reason: "unexpected_payload_keys"`.
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — Sentry escalation on the shape-mismatch branch of `fetchStrategyLazyMetrics` (array / primitive payloads). The error-channel was already Sentry-tagged via H-0488; the shape-mismatch path previously logged to `console.error` only, so a SECURITY DEFINER return-type drift was invisible to alerting.
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — module constants `POSITION_SIZE_ZERO_TOLERANCE_USD` (1e-9), `WIN_RATE_PERCENT_HEURISTIC_THRESHOLD` (1.5), `SQN_TRADE_COUNT_CAP` (100). The rationale (NUMERIC residual tolerance, ULP-drift-at-100%-winners boundary, Van Tharp's original cap) lives at the constant definition site once instead of being repeated in three scattered inline comments.
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — `except PaginatedSelectTruncated` handler above the broad `except Exception` in `run_strategy_analytics`. The typed exception was previously swallowed into a generic HTTPException(500); the worker dispatcher then classified the 500 as "unknown" and retried indefinitely on a permanent data-shape fault. Now persists a truncation-specific `computation_error` (page count + hint) and re-raises so callers can classify as permanent.
- `analytics-service/routers/match.py` — cron-recompute status discriminator. Emits `"total_failure"` when processed=0 + failed>0 (structural fault), `"degraded"` when failures outnumber successes, `"ok"` only when failures don't outnumber successes. Operators with dashboards switching on `status` now see structural faults instead of green-on-broken.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_analytics_runner.py` — 3 new regression tests: (1) `PaginatedSelectTruncated` propagation through `run_strategy_analytics` with truncation-specific `computation_error`, (2) helper-level truncation raises typed exception, (3) win_rate=1.0001 ULP drift stays fractional (pins `WIN_RATE_PERCENT_HEURISTIC_THRESHOLD` at 1.5).
- `analytics-service/tests/test_match_router.py` — 5 new regression tests: cron `total_failure` + `degraded` statuses, orphan-rollback insert-raises path (with `__cause__` chaining), cleanup-failure path (rollback delete itself raising must not mask insert error), engine_version mismatch forces recompute within the 12h age guard.
- `src/lib/queries.test.ts` — 7 new tests: Sentry capture on RPC error (with `rpc_code` tag, defaulting to `"unknown"` when error.code absent), Sentry on shape-mismatch (`reason: "unexpected_payload_shape"`), no-log on legitimate null visibility-miss, unexpected-keys filter, non-string-but-truthy `strategy_id` filter (numbers/booleans/arrays/objects dropped), data:null+error:null RLS-block edge case returns empty Set.
- `src/lib/queries.audit-2026-05-07.test.ts` — 4 new tests: every BridgeOutcome field propagates verbatim, throws on missing required `id` (was: silently `{ id: undefined }`), throws on invalid `kind` enum value, `needs_recompute` non-boolean truthy stays false, candidate strategies SELECT error throws via assertOk instead of silently emptying flaggedHoldings.
- `src/lib/analytics-schemas.test.ts` — `RecomputeMatchResponseSchema` negative tests: rejects unknown statuses (`throttled`, `total_failure`, `degraded`, etc.), missing status field, while preserving `.passthrough()` for forward-compat fields.
- `src/__tests__/outcomes-join-rls.test.ts` — mock `@/lib/supabase/server` with a mutable `authMock.userId` so the H-0502 auth backstop can be exercised in live-DB tests; new cross-tenant exfiltration probe (alloc2 signed in, calls `getMyAllocationDashboard(alloc1)` — must throw `userId does not match authenticated user`).

### Changed
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `_PanelMapChecked` anchor restored. The H-0496 type-level invariant guard was a bare type alias that TypeScript lazy-evaluates; a value drift in `LazyMetricsPanelKindMap` would compile silently. New `const _panelMapInvariant: _PanelMapChecked = true` forces evaluation at a real assignment site.
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `normalizeEmbed` replaces `as EmbeddedStrategyRaw` cast with runtime narrowing. Non-string `id`, non-object embeds, or array-of-corrupt-objects collapse to null instead of shipping `{ id: undefined, name: ... }` to OutcomeRow consumers (same bug class as H-0490).
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `pickBridgeOutcomeFields` replaces every `row.X as Y` cast with strict typeof helpers (`requiredString`, `nullableString`, `nullableNumber`, `isBridgeKind`, `isRejectionReason` using the shared `REJECTION_REASONS` const from bridge-outcome-schema). A SELECT column drop now throws `missing required <field>` at the boundary; an invalid kind throws `invalid kind <value>`; nullable fields type-narrow before assignment.
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `needs_recompute` now uses strict `=== true` comparison instead of `Boolean()` coercion. `Boolean("false")` is `true` in JavaScript, so a column-type drift (BOOLEAN → TEXT) would have flipped the stale-data UI indicator on every row.
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — candidate-strategies SELECT in `getMyAllocationDashboard` flows through `assertOk`. Previously destructured `{ data }`-only, so a Supabase error silently produced empty `nameById` → every flagged holding's `name` resolved to undefined → the filter stripped EVERY entry. Allocators saw an empty flagged-holdings panel masking real breach signals.
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — `_load_position_time_series` drops the `account_balance` parameter from its signature entirely. C-0221 was previously enforced by "value not consumed below"; removing the parameter makes the leak impossible by construction.
- `analytics-service/services/db.py` — `PaginatedSelectTruncated` docstring no longer claims hints "never include UUIDs". Two analytics_runner callers correctly pass strategy UUIDs; the new docstring documents the trade-off explicitly (single-strategy UUIDs allowed for operator triage, multi-tenant/PII payloads are not).
- `analytics-service/tests/test_match_router.py` — `client` TestClient fixture promoted from function-scoped to module-scoped. FastAPI app construction now happens once per test file instead of per test method.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_match_router.py` — dead `_utc` helper deleted (defined but never called).

### Fixed
- `src/lib/queries.ts` — `fetchStrategyLazyMetrics` null-data log restored. A `data === null` (legitimate visibility miss per the function's documented contract) no longer logs as "unexpected RPC payload shape" — only undefined/primitive/array do. Previously every private-strategy panel load spammed the error stream with false alarms.

## [0.22.36.0] - 2026-05-15

**audit-2026-05-07 — analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py audit closure (specialist + red-team round).** Closes 8 H-tier audit findings (H-0733, H-0736, H-0738, H-0741, H-0743, H-0744, H-0745, H-0747) on the FIFO position-reconstruction + exposure-metrics + turnover-series pipeline. A subsequent /simplify pass + 5-specialist fan-out (correctness / security / type / testing / quality) + red-team adversarial review surfaced 3 additional medium+ issues, all addressed here. Allocator UI experience: VolumeExposureTab no longer renders spurious zero exposure when position_snapshots haven't been collected yet; turnover charts now distinguish "NAV not yet ingested" (rendered as null) from "real margin-call NAV<=0" (rendered as zero) from "genuine no-rebalances day" (computed); dashboards surface `duration_parse_errors` instead of silently dropping malformed fill timestamps; `realized_pnl_per_trade` / `exposure_series` / 4 turnover flag-lists are size-capped with sibling counter keys so a fill-flood or grid-flood can't bloat strategy_analytics JSONB. Operators get explicit Railway log signals for non-finite / overflow / Decimal-NaN inputs instead of silent JSONB poisoning.

### Added
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `RealizedPnLRecord` + `ExposurePoint` TypedDicts pin the wire shape of two unbounded-by-input JSONB payloads (H-0733 / H-0743); pre-fix both were typed as bare `list[dict[str, Any]]`, so a producer typo like `side='LONG'` would silently bucket into the wrong long/short profit-factor downstream.
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `_coerce_finite_float(value, label)` module-level helper consolidates `float()` + `math.isfinite()` rejection for the 3 turnover-series coercion sites (positions / prices / NAV). Catches `OverflowError` from `float(huge_int)` and re-raises as `ValueError` so a poisoned position value drops just that one date instead of aborting the whole series.
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `_emit_capped_flag_list(flags, name, lst)` module-level helper caps any data-quality flag list at `_FLAG_LIST_CAP=1000` and emits sibling counter keys (`*_truncated`, `*_truncated_kept`, `*_truncated_total`) matching the convention already used at the realized_pnl_per_trade and exposure_series cap sites.
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `_REALIZED_PNL_PER_TRADE_CAP=10_000`, `_EXPOSURE_SERIES_CAP=10_000`, `_FLAG_LIST_CAP=1_000` cardinality caps on the unbounded JSONB payloads persisted to strategy_analytics (H-0736 red-team).
- `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py` — single-producer invariant on `*_truncated_kept` / `*_truncated_total` counter keys documented in `_merge_into_top_level_flags` docstring; the int-sum merge rule would silently corrupt kept-counts under cross-producer key collision.
- `analytics-service/tests/test_position_reconstruction.py` — 5 new tests: 2 OverflowError regressions (position + NAV paths drop just the bad date instead of aborting), 3 parameterized truncation-cap tests covering `turnover_nav_invalid_dates` / `turnover_gap_dates` / `turnover_series_dropped_dates` (previously only `turnover_nav_missing_dates` exercised the cap helper).

### Changed
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `compute_exposure_metrics` no longer returns bare `{}` on no-snapshots or apikey-lookup failure (H-0747 + H-0738); always surfaces a `data_quality_flags` marker so VolumeExposureTab can show "Position snapshots not yet collected" instead of spurious zeros, and security reviewers can discriminate the three contamination-guard failure modes (no shared key / shared key skipped / lookup failed).
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `compute_turnover_series_with_flags` differentiates NAV-missing (`turnover=None` + `turnover_nav_missing_dates` flag) from NAV-invalid (`turnover=0.0` + `turnover_nav_invalid_dates` flag) from genuine no-rebalances day (H-0744); both gap paths preserve `prev_positions` so the next valid date computes a true delta across the gap rather than seeing a phantom no-op.
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — duration-parse failures in `_match_positions_fifo` now log + increment a `duration_parse_errors` counter on the position's `data_quality_flags` instead of silently swallowing the exception (H-0745); allocators can now tell "duration unknown due to bad fill timestamp" apart from "still-open position".
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — 4× inline `x != x or x in (float("inf"), float("-inf"))` NaN/Inf checks replaced with `math.isfinite()` via the new `_coerce_finite_float` helper (matches codebase convention used in `match_engine.py`, `metrics.py`, `equity_reconstruction.py`).
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `dq_flags` accumulator in `compute_exposure_metrics` consolidated from three local rebindings into a single accumulator at the top of the data-quality phase; deleted the `if not gross_exposures:` unreachable defensive branch (the loop unconditionally appends to `gross_exposures`).
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — return type of `compute_turnover_series_with_flags` widened from `tuple[list[dict], dict[str, list[str]]]` → `tuple[list[dict], dict[str, Any]]` to accommodate the bool/int sibling counter keys; `_merge_into_top_level_flags` (the sole consumer) already type-dispatches on bool/int/list, so the change is transparent downstream.

### Fixed
- `analytics-service/services/position_reconstruction.py` — `compute_turnover_series_with_flags` no longer aborts the entire loop on a single non-finite position / price / NAV value (H-0741 + specialist OverflowError follow-up); the bad date lands in `turnover_series_dropped_dates` and the loop continues without advancing `prev_positions` so the next valid date measures a true delta across the gap.

## [0.22.35.0] - 2026-05-15

**audit-2026-05-07 — analytics-service/metrics.py audit closure (specialist + red-team round).** Closes 13 H-tier audit findings (H-0710..H-0730) on the `compute_all_metrics` pipeline. A subsequent /simplify pass + 5-specialist fan-out (security / correctness / performance / testing / maintainability) + Claude red-team adversarial review surfaced 14 additional issues, all addressed here. Analytics dashboards now stay continuous through upstream gap days, payloads are uniformly NaN/Inf-scrubbed and size-capped, and the `time_in_market` indicator no longer counts gap days as "in market". Allocator UI experience: equity curves stop silently truncating at the first missing benchmark day, the heat-map renderer gets a stable bounded daily-returns payload, and operators get explicit Railway log signals for upstream data corruption (returns ≤ -1, NaN gaps, qstats version drift, rolling_greeks column drift) instead of silent metric degradation.

### Added
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `_drop_nonfinite(series)` shared helper consolidates the NaN/Inf scrub pattern across every series writer (Postgres JSONB rejects NaN — the H-0715-class chokepoint).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `_format_series_points(series, decimals)` vectorized `{date, value}` dict construction with `DatetimeIndex` assertion and `strict=True` zip (fail-loud on length mismatch, ~10× faster strftime per call, byte-stable Python-decimal rounding).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `_safe_qstats_scalar(name, fn, returns, returns_len)` helper + module-level `_QSTATS_SINGLE_ARG_SCALARS` dispatch table replace 10 near-identical try/except blocks in `compute_qstats_scalars` (~60 LOC of dup removed; single logging contract; `getattr` resolved at call time so existing monkeypatch tests still work).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `_rolling_sortino_from_components(returns, neg_sq, window)` window-parameterized inner that lets the three Sortino windows (63 / 126 / 252) share a single `neg_sq = (returns.where(returns < MAR, 0.0)) ** 2` materialization (H-0721 hot-path fix).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `_rolling_alpha_beta(returns, benchmark, window)` returns both projections from ONE `qs.stats.rolling_greeks` regression pass (was 2× the work), plus inner-join alignment of the returns/benchmark indexes and a structured WARNING when qs returns a DataFrame missing the expected alpha/beta columns (H-0711 / H-0726.1 / .2 / .3).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `SeriesPoint` TypedDict mirrors the React-side `{date: string, value: number}` contract so a renamed key surfaces at type-check time instead of as runtime NaN (H-0730).
- `analytics-service/services/metrics.py` — `MAR=0.0` and `_LOG_RETURN_FLOOR=-1.0+1e-9` module constants clustered at the top.
- 25+ regression tests covering every H-XXXX finding plus the specialist + red-team follow-ups: divide-by-zero RuntimeWarning suppression on all-winning windows, NaN/Inf scrub on the daily-returns grid, payload cap at 5000 points, single-`rolling_greeks` pass invariant, single-`neg_sq` materialization, alpha/beta inner-join alignment, short / zero-overlap benchmark short-circuit, qs.stats.rolling_greeks failure logging, `MetricsResult` sibling-kind subscript guard, cumulative log equity contract + catastrophic-loss preservation, parametrized failure path across all 8 dispatch entries, `r_squared_status` ∈ {`no_benchmark`, `ok`, `error`} disambiguation including the NaN-from-qs path, `time_in_market` NaN exclusion regression.

### Changed
- `compute_all_metrics` — chart-feeding paths (`(1+returns).cumprod()`, `qs.stats.to_drawdown_series`, benchmark `bm_cumulative`) now consume `returns.fillna(0)` so a single upstream gap day doesn't silently truncate the entire equity curve at the gap (red-team F3). Raw `returns` still flows to `qs.stats.*` for statistics so NaN exclusion remains in the indicators that want it.
- `compute_all_metrics` — emits `logger.warning` at entry when `returns` contains NaN or any `returns <= -1.0` (catastrophic-loss / oscillating-equity precondition; surfaces upstream-data corruption to Railway logs).
- `compute_qstats_scalars` — `time_in_market` switched from `qs.stats.exposure` (which ceil-rounds UP to the nearest 1% — 1/252 displayed as 1% instead of 0.4%) to `(returns.notna() & (returns != 0)).sum() / len(returns)`, mirroring quantstats's NaN-exclusion predicate exactly so gap days don't inflate the fraction (H-0724 + red-team correctness fix).
- `compute_qstats_scalars` — `r_squared_status` is now `"ok"` only when `_safe_float` returns a non-None value; `"error"` when qs returned NaN/Inf or raised; `"no_benchmark"` is the default. Status no longer lies when qs returns a non-finite r_squared (red-team F7).
- `compute_qstats_scalars` — every qs.stats failure path now emits `logger.warning(name=..., returns_len=..., exc_info=True)` instead of `except: pass`. Converts "10 scalars silently degrade to None" into an operator-visible Railway signal (H-0710 / H-0713 / H-0723).
- `qs.stats.sortino` is now called with `rf=MAR` explicitly so the scalar Sortino and `_rolling_sortino` share the SAME minimum acceptable return constant; relying on the qs default `rf=0` silently diverged the moment MAR was tuned (H-0725).
- `_daily_returns_grid_from_series` — now routes through `_drop_nonfinite` + `cap_data_points` (was an unscrubbed comprehension that could leak NaN into the atomic sibling-batch upsert and a 10y backtest's 2,520 rows bypassed the 5000-point cap that every other helper enforces) (H-0715 / H-0720).
- `_log_returns_series` — returns cumulative log equity (`np.log1p(returns).cumsum()`) instead of per-period log returns so the EquityCurve "Log Returns" toggle renders monotonically on a log axis instead of noise around zero (H-0719). Catastrophic-loss days (r ≤ -1, e.g. liquidation events) are clamped to `_LOG_RETURN_FLOOR = -1 + 1e-9` before `log1p` so they surface as `~-20.72` rather than vanishing through `_finalize_rolling.dropna()` (H-0728).
- `_rolling_sortino_from_components` — explicit divide-by-zero guard via `np.where(roll_dstd > 0, ratio, np.nan)`. Previously an all-winning window (every observation ≥ MAR=0 → `roll_dstd == 0`) emitted a divide-by-zero `RuntimeWarning` AND produced ±Inf points that `_finalize_rolling` silently scrubbed — wiping the windows where the strategy performed BEST off the chart (H-0712 / H-0716).
- `_finalize_rolling` + `_daily_returns_grid_from_series` — switched to the shared `_drop_nonfinite` + `_format_series_points` helpers. Eliminates a double-`dropna()` in `_finalize_rolling`, vectorizes per-row `strftime + round(float, n)` (estimated ~30-40ms saved per analytics refresh across the 10× per-strategy invocation), keeps Python-decimal rounding semantics (NOT NumPy banker rounding — preserves byte-stable stored JSONB across the refactor).
- `MetricsResult.__getitem__` — sibling-kind subscript now raises a descriptive `KeyError` pointing to `result.sibling_kinds[...]`. Previously the bare proxy let a `result.sibling_kinds[k] → result[k]` refactor look correct in review while silently `KeyError`ing in production for every split-storage kind (H-0727).
- `compute_qstats_scalars` — adds `r_squared_status` companion key so operators can disambiguate "benchmark missing" from "qs raised" from "qs returned NaN" without trawling logs (H-0718).

### Fixed
- HIGH (red-team carry-over from H-0715): `metrics_json["benchmark_returns"]` previously skipped the same NaN/Inf scrub + `cap_data_points` chokepoint as every other series — a single benchmark gap day plus the absent payload cap could either reject the JSONB upsert OR balloon a 10y BTC benchmark series past the 5000-row budget. Now scrubs + caps uniformly with `cap_data_points(_format_series_points(_drop_nonfinite(bm_cumulative), 6))`.
- MEDIUM: `returns_series` and `drawdown_series` defense-in-depth NaN/Inf scrub at the helper boundary (was relying on the `sanitize_metrics` tail to catch JSONB-rejecting NaN; one upstream gap day through `(1+returns).cumprod()` propagated NaN to every subsequent row).
- MEDIUM (correctness): `time_in_market` previously inflated the fraction whenever upstream CSV gaps injected NaN because `pandas.Series.NaN != 0` evaluates to `True`. Switched to the NaN-aware predicate `(returns.notna() & (returns != 0))`.
- MEDIUM (Python 3.10+ safety): `_format_series_points` now asserts `isinstance(series.index, pd.DatetimeIndex)` and `zip(..., strict=True)` guards length mismatch — fail-loud on contract violations instead of silent truncation.

### Removed
- Duplicate NaN/Inf scrub + per-row formatting code paths from `_finalize_rolling` and `_daily_returns_grid_from_series` (consolidated into the shared helpers above).
- 10 near-identical try/except blocks in `compute_qstats_scalars` (replaced by `_safe_qstats_scalar` + dispatch table).
- Redundant `float(...)` casts in `time_in_market` (numpy true-division already returns float).

## [0.22.34.6] - 2026-05-15

**audit-2026-05-07 — analytics-runner.py audit closure (specialist + red-team round).** Closes the C-0221 tenant-balance leak via `turnover_series` and 10 H-tier findings against `analytics-service/services/analytics_runner.py`. A subsequent specialist fan-out (security / financial-math / concurrency / schema / test-quality) and red-team adversarial review surfaced 14 additional issues, all addressed in this version. Highlights: shared `_paginated_select` helper consolidates two duplicated pagination loops and now raises `PaginatedSelectTruncated` (fail-loud) on real overflow while correctly returning on the exact-boundary case; composite-tuple `order_by` for both snapshot and fills pagination eliminates cross-page row instability on non-unique sort keys; SQN citation corrected (Van Tharp textbook, NOT quantstats parity — quantstats has no SQN); weighted R:R docstring clarified as deliberately-non-canonical; `math.isfinite` boundary guard added on `avg_win` / `avg_loss` consumed by expectancy; `win_rate` boundary clamp tightened to `> 1.5` so ULP drift at 100% winners cannot silently divide by 100. Allocator-side TS interface gains `fills_missing_is_maker_pct?: number;` so the new DQF has a typed home.

### Added
- `analytics-service/services/db.py` — shared `_paginated_select(builder, order_by, ...)` helper and `PaginatedSelectTruncated` exception class, moved from `match_eval.py` so both `analytics_runner.py` and `match_eval.py` use a single implementation.
- Test additions: `test_paginated_select_no_false_positive_at_exact_boundary` (db), `test_derived_trade_metrics_sqn_caps_at_sqrt_100` (N=50 vs N=200), `test_fills_missing_is_maker_pct_rounds_to_four_decimals` (1/7 → 0.1429), `test_fills_missing_is_maker_pct_omitted_when_zero`, plus an `order_calls` assertion on the snapshot pagination test pinning the composite `(snapshot_date, symbol, side)` order_by contract.
- `AnalyticsDataQualityFlags.fills_missing_is_maker_pct?: number;` in `src/lib/types.ts` so frontend consumers can render the new DQF.

### Changed
- `_load_position_time_series` paginates via `_paginated_select` with composite `order_by=(("snapshot_date", False), ("symbol", False), ("side", False))` — matches `position_snapshots_unique_per_day` from migration 034 so cross-page row ties cannot duplicate or skip.
- `run_strategy_analytics` fills fetch paginates via `_paginated_select` with composite `order_by=(("timestamp", False), ("id", False))` — OKX millisecond timestamps collide trivially and the UUID PK is a guaranteed tiebreaker.
- `_paginated_select` now peeks one extra page before raising `PaginatedSelectTruncated` so a dataset of EXACTLY `hard_cap_pages × page_size` rows returns the full set instead of false-positive truncating.
- Both snapshot and fills paths re-raise `PaginatedSelectTruncated` before the broad `except Exception` so audit #52's fail-loud intent is honored (the broad except previously downgraded truncation to a generic `SNAPSHOTS_LOAD_FAILED` / `FILLS_FETCH_FAILED` DQF, conflating scale overflow with transient failure).
- `_compute_derived_trade_metrics`: `win_rate` boundary tightened to `> 1.5` (was `> 1.0`) so ULP drift at exactly 1.0 doesn't get divided by 100; `math.isfinite` boundary guard added for `avg_win` / `avg_loss` (zeroed independently so a healthy partner metric isn't wiped when only one side is non-finite); SQN comment corrected to cite Van Tharp's textbook rather than fabricated "quantstats parity"; weighted R:R docstring acknowledges the formula is deliberately not canonical Van Tharp.
- `account_balance_unavailable` docstring clarified — post-C-0221, the flag is informational only (analytics no longer consumes `account_balance` for NAV regardless of the flag's value).

### Removed
- Duplicate pagination loops (~60 lines) replaced by `_paginated_select` calls.
- Misleading "Forward-reference is fine — Python resolves at call time" comment (the helper is in fact defined BEFORE its uses).
- Dead `else 0.0` branch in the weighted-R:R walrus expression.

## [0.22.34.5] - 2026-05-15

**audit-2026-05-07 — match router hardening + first router test suite.** Closes the C-0203 kill-chain (kill-switch fail-open + silent cron 200-OK + retention sweep cascade + blocking event loop), the C-0204 demo-allocator leak (real published strategies surfacing through the anon `/api/demo/match` endpoint), and 14 H-tier findings against `analytics-service/routers/match.py`. The new `analytics-service/tests/test_match_router.py` is the first test file to exercise the FastAPI surface (POST `/api/match/recompute`, GET `/api/match/eval`, POST `/api/match/cron-recompute`) — 30 cases covering kill-switch fail-open, skip/empty/exception branches, retention-sweep slice + ordering invariants, cron partial-failure isolation, total-failure structural logging, mandate parse-failure recompute, orphan-batch rollback, and the demo-only universe filter.

### Added
- `analytics-service/tests/test_match_router.py` (30 cases) exercising the recompute, eval, and cron-recompute endpoints plus the `_kill_switch_enabled`, `_should_skip_allocator`, `_retention_sweep`, and `_score_one_allocator` helpers.
- `RecomputeMatchResponseSchema` now requires a `status` discriminator (`"disabled" | "skipped" | "ok"`) so callers can switch on a single field.

### Changed
- `_score_one_allocator` off-loads `score_candidates` AND `compute_holding_flags` to threads so pandas/numpy correlation work no longer blocks the FastAPI event loop. `_ScoredProxy` lifted out of the hot path to module scope.
- `cron_recompute` returns a unified response shape on every early-return branch (`disabled`, `no_allocators`, `empty_universe`, `ok`) — every branch carries `status`, `processed`, `skipped`, `failed`, `retention_deleted`, and `duration_s`.
- `_retention_sweep` paginates DELETEs to ≤ 50 IDs per request so the PostgREST IN-list URL stays under any reasonable cap.
- `_should_skip_allocator` checks the age guard BEFORE consulting `mandate_edited_at`, saving O(allocators) Supabase round-trips per cron run.
- `RecomputeRequest.allocator_id` is typed as `UUID` so the request boundary rejects malformed input with a 422 instead of round-tripping a 0-row Supabase result.

### Fixed
- **Kill-switch failure now logs at ERROR** so Supabase blips light up Sentry. The fail-open contract is preserved (engine keeps running on transient DB faults).
- **Cron returns a TOTAL FAILURE error log** when every allocator fails (processed=0, failed>0) — previously a structural failure (schema drift, KEK missing) was silently 200-OK to the cron scheduler.
- **Per-allocator failure isolation** in the cron loop. One bad allocator no longer aborts the daily recompute; failed/processed/skipped counters are honest.
- **Mandate parse-failure** previously swallowed with bare `pass`, silently downgrading the "mandate edit invalidates cached batch" trigger into a no-op. Now logs a WARNING and forces a recompute.
- **Orphan-batch rollback**: when the `match_candidates` insert fails or returns empty, the parent `match_batches` row is deleted so the admin queue never sees a `candidate_count > 0` row with zero children. The original insert exception is preserved via `raise from err` for triage.
- **Retention sweep failure after a successful insert** no longer 500s the request — the batch already landed; log loudly and return the result.
- **Demo-allocator universe** is post-filtered to `is_example=true` strategies only, plugging the leak where real published strategies could surface through the anon-public `/api/demo/match` endpoint.
- **`eval` route boundary** now returns a structured 503 with page-count + page-size context when `_paginated_select` hits its hard cap (was a generic 500 that silently aggregated over a partial window).

## [0.22.34.4] - 2026-05-15

**phase-19 PR-Y1 — backfill audit-2026-05-07 schema + rename migrations to timestamp convention.** Production was silently missing ~11 migrations of hardening because the supabase-migrate workflow had been failing on every push to main since the migration-naming drift began (~May 12). This release closes the gap (live on prod) and reconciles local + remote naming so the workflow can be re-enabled cleanly after PR-Y2.

The most consequential fixes now live on prod: migration 117 (CRITICAL P97/G12.A.2 `compute_jobs.claim_token` race fence + 5 RPC replacements — mark RPCs verify the fencing token to detect watchdog preemption), migration 124 (CRITICAL P455 `data_deletion_requests.user_id` FK changed from `ON DELETE CASCADE` to `ON DELETE SET NULL` so GDPR DSR audit trails survive user deletion), and migration 127 (audit-2026-05-07 red-team Findings 1/3/4/8: replaces a privesc-vulnerable GUC bypass design with an unforgeable `current_user` check on `guard_wizard_draft_updates` + `reject_sentinel_writes`). 127 supersedes intermediate migrations 120 (sentinel triggers), 125 (auth.uid hardening), and 126 (GUC bypass design). All five (120/125/126/127) ship as separate row entries to record full history, but the chronologically last body installed by `supabase db push` is 127's hardened end-state — the timestamp prefixes for 120/125/126 sort strictly before 127 (`20260513…` < `20260515114310`), so fresh-DB rebuilds and `supabase db reset` always land the hardened bodies last. This is the gate the security specialist review caught and that 0.22.34.4's fix-loop addressed.

Migration 117 was applied as three rows to fit the MCP gateway's apply-time budget then consolidated into a single `compute_jobs_claim_token_fencing` row. Migration 127 was similarly split into 127a (`guard_wizard_draft_updates`) + 127b (`reject_sentinel_writes` + 3 triggers) and consolidated. 127c/d/e (function bodies for `create_wizard_strategy` / `finalize_wizard_strategy` / `sanitize_user`) are no-ops on prod because the never-applied 126 didn't introduce the GUC `set_config` calls 127c/d/e were meant to remove — verified via direct probing against `pg_get_functiondef`.

Migrations 047b (`used_ack_tokens`), 118 (`_enqueue_compute_job_internal` ACL), 130 (`scenario_commit_idempotency`), and 131 (`commit_scenario_batch` idempotency parameter) had schema effects already on prod but no `schema_migrations` rows — INSERTed rows to record reality. 77 orphan `audit_log.user_id` values (rows pointing to deleted `auth.users`) were NULLed before 123's FK validation; this is exactly what `ON DELETE SET NULL` would have done historically had the FK existed at delete time.

129 local migration files renamed from `NNN_*.sql` to `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_*.sql` to match Supabase CLI conventions, plus 7 rollback files in `down/`. Timestamps derived from `git log --diff-filter=A --follow` UTC first-commit time, with collision-resolution by NNN order. `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` on prod reconciled in the same transaction (80 NNN→TS UPDATEs, 5 NNN-vs-TS duplicate DELETEs, 7 INSERT-only rows, 2 INSERT rows for the deferred 107/119 — see deferred-apply warnings below).

Migrations 107 (`verification_requests` view shim) and 119 (`positions_natural_key` UNIQUE constraint) carry **deferred-apply warning blocks** at the top of their SQL bodies. Both have `schema_migrations` rows on prod claiming "applied" but their SQL has NOT actually run on prod. 107 is deferred because its M-5 preflight assertion needs revalidation against post-PR-X5 prod data and `sanitize_user`'s `DELETE FROM verification_requests` needs to be updated to target `verification_requests_legacy`. 119 is deferred because 8 duplicate `(strategy_id, symbol, side, opened_at)` groups exist on prod that the constraint install would reject — PR-Y2 will run `reconstruct_positions_atomic` first then install the constraint via a new filename. Future devs MUST NOT remove these rows without writing replacement migrations; the warnings are explicit about this.

The `.github/workflows/supabase-migrate.yml` `paths:` auto-trigger is stripped — workflow now runs only on `workflow_dispatch`. The inline comment names the four explicit re-enable conditions: (1) PR-Y2 has shipped 107 + 119, (2) test project schema_migrations is reconciled, (3) a manual `workflow_dispatch` shows `supabase db push` as a true no-op, (4) Phase 19 soak window has closed without rollback.

The reconcile SQL and audit summary are committed to `docs/runbooks/pr-y1-schema-migrations-reconcile.sql` + `docs/runbooks/pr-y1-audit-summary.md` so the forensic record survives a fresh checkout (the `.planning/phase-19/audit/` working artifacts are gitignored).

### Fixed
- **chronological inversion of 120/125/126 vs 127** — the initial PR-Y1 commit used today's date (`20260515130002`–`20260515130004`) for migrations 120/125/126 because no prior `schema_migrations` row existed to derive timestamps from. Those timestamps sort AFTER 127 (`20260515114310`), so `supabase db push` against a fresh DB would have run 127 first and then 120/125/126 — overwriting 127's hardened bodies with the vulnerable GUC versions. Renamed to git-derived UTC timestamps (`20260513073518`, `20260513082230`, `20260513084844`) that sort correctly before 127. Audit-2026-05-07 red-team Findings 1/3/4/8 now stay closed across every fresh-DB rebuild path (DR, test-project reseed, `supabase db reset`).
- **bare-filename references missed by initial find-replace** — the first pass only matched paths with the `supabase/migrations/` prefix. Bare filenames in backticks (e.g. `` `011_perfect_match.sql` ``), wildcards (`129_*.sql`), and `down/NNN-rollback.sql` rollback references in `audit/`, `docs/architecture/` ADRs, `docs/runbooks/` (match-engine, bridge-outcome-cron), `CHANGELOG.md`, and test path constants were left stale. Active runbook `docs/runbooks/match-engine.md` would have broken at step 1 for any new founder following the deploy flow. Updated 20 additional files via a second find-replace pass with broader patterns.
- **107/119 SQL files now carry deferred-apply warning blocks at the top** — the original PR-Y1 commit inserted "applied" rows for these two migrations on prod but never ran their SQL bodies (see deferred-apply rationale above). Without an in-source warning, the next engineer running `supabase db diff` or attempting to re-apply via the dashboard would see the row already exists and skip, leaving the schema permanently missing the view shim / natural key constraint with no trace in the migration source. This is the failure shape the audit-2026-05-07 series was meant to prevent.
- **reconcile SQL promoted out of gitignored `.planning/`** to `docs/runbooks/pr-y1-schema-migrations-reconcile.sql`. The 92-statement reconciliation that mutated prod's `schema_migrations` now survives a fresh clone.

## [0.22.34.3] - 2026-05-15

**phase-19 — unify teaser into the /process-key backbone (PR-X5).** Two abortive PR-B kill-switch flip attempts on 2026-05-14 confirmed teaser submissions to `/process-key` were returning `MISSING_STRATEGY_ID` 422 — the unified backend had no story for `flow_type='teaser'` without a caller-owned strategy. PR-X5 makes the unified backbone the single path for teaser too: the dispatch in `analytics-service/routers/process_key.py` now injects a sentinel anchor `strategy_id` (provisioned by new migration 132) plus a fresh `wizard_session_id` for `flow_type='teaser'` BEFORE the audit row write, then falls through to the existing synchronous pipeline at line 684 unchanged. Zero `if flow_type=='teaser'` branches downstream. Backend is one path. Same logic for every flow.

The TS unified handler at `src/app/api/verify-strategy/route.ts` is hardened to allowlist the context fields forwarded upstream — `{email, exchange, api_key, api_secret, passphrase?}` only, never the raw request body. Defense in depth: the Python dispatch also OVERRIDES `strategy_id`/`wizard_session_id` unconditionally for teaser (not fill-if-null), so a future TS regression that spread the raw body can't reintroduce the cross-tenant write where an attacker pre-supplies a victim's `strategy_id`.

The unified pipeline now enriches `metrics_snapshot` with the legacy `verify_strategy` response shape — `return_24h/return_mtd/return_ytd`, `equity_curve`, and `matched_strategy_id` — by reusing `services.transforms.trades_to_daily_returns`, `services.portfolio_metrics.compute_period_returns`, and the new `services.strategy_matching.find_matched_strategy` (extracted from `portfolio.py:1024-1051`). This runs for every flow_type so the SV row's `metrics_snapshot` column has the same shape regardless of where the row was created. Enrichment falls back to heuristic-capital returns when account_balance isn't available (same as the legacy path), and the whole block is best-effort — a math error logs `process_key.enrichment_failed` and ships the base shape rather than failing the verification.

The TS legacy handler swaps its "anchor to most recent strategies row" SELECT — a documented privacy leak per migration 107 DM-3 — for the same `TEASER_ANCHOR_STRATEGY_ID` constant. One source of truth, mirrored in `src/lib/phase-19-constants.ts` (TS) and `analytics-service/services/teaser_anchor.py` (Python), pinned by a new `tests/integration/phase-19-constants-sync.test.ts` drift guard.

The landing-page progress component now accepts `status='published'` (the unified pipeline's terminal state) in addition to the legacy `'complete'`. Pre-X5 the public-status poll surfaced the new status but the React `VerificationProgress` had `'complete' | 'failed'` hardcoded in its union — teaser users on the unified path would have seen eternal "processing" until the 5-minute poll timeout, then "failed."

### Added

- **Migration 132 — teaser-anchor sentinel** (`supabase/migrations/20260515095804_teaser_anchor_strategy.sql`). Seeds a permanent system pseudo-user at `auth.users.id='00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'` (NULL password, non-routable `system-phase-19-sentinel@quantalyze.internal` email — sign-in path closed), a matching `profiles` row, and the sentinel strategy at `id='00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001'` with `status='archived'` so it never surfaces in marketplace queries. All three INSERTs idempotent via `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING` and gated by a self-verify `DO $$ … $$` block that raises if any row is missing post-INSERT. Mirror rollback at `supabase/migrations/down/132-rollback.sql` deletes in reverse-FK order.
- **`TEASER_ANCHOR_STRATEGY_ID` shared constant** (`src/lib/phase-19-constants.ts` for TS, `analytics-service/services/teaser_anchor.py` for Python).
- **`find_matched_strategy` shared helper** (`analytics-service/services/strategy_matching.py`). Extracted from the inline correlation block in legacy `verify_strategy` so both the legacy endpoint and the unified pipeline use one match implementation. 95% correlation threshold + 30-day minimum overlap window preserved verbatim.
- **3 regression tests + 1 drift guard.** `analytics-service/tests/test_migration_132.py` pins the migration's INSERTs against the Python constant (7 static-AST checks). `test_process_key.py::test_process_key_teaser_injects_anchor_when_strategy_id_missing` pins the dispatch contract end-to-end. `tests/integration/process-key-thin-adapters.test.ts` replaces the PR-X3 forward `step='validate'` test with the inverse PR-X5 "context does NOT include step" test. `tests/integration/phase-19-constants-sync.test.ts` pins TS ↔ Python ↔ SQL drift.

### Changed

- **`analytics-service/routers/process_key.py`** — dispatch injection for teaser before audit-row write; unified pipeline's `metrics_captured` transition now persists the enriched `metrics_snapshot` (period returns + equity curve + matched_strategy_id) instead of the bare `MetricsSnapshot` dataclass; top-level response shape gains `matched_strategy_id` for legacy parity.
- **`src/app/api/verify-strategy/route.ts`** — unified handler context allowlist (no raw body spread); legacy handler swaps "most recent strategies row" SELECT for the `TEASER_ANCHOR_STRATEGY_ID` constant.
- **`src/components/landing/VerificationSection.tsx`** + `VerificationProgress.tsx` — `Status` union extended with `'published'`; success transition accepts both `'complete'` and `'published'`.

### Removed

- The PR-X3 forward test `verify-strategy: teaser context forwards step='validate'` (replaced with the inverse contract).
- The pra-write graceful-degrade test for "no anchor strategies row" (the dynamic SELECT it gated on is gone; the migration's self-verify DO block is now the load-bearing guarantee).

## [0.22.34.2] - 2026-05-15

**phase-19 — teaser legacy path writes terminal SV row with metrics (PR-X4a).** Pre-fix the legacy `teaserVerifyStrategyHandler` upserted `strategy_verifications` at `status='validated'` with no `metrics_snapshot` column. The public-status route at `verify-strategy/[id]/status/route.ts:107` only returns `results` when status is `'complete'` (legacy VR shape) or `'published'` (canonical SV terminal), so polling that row returned `{status:'validated'}` with no score. Teaser users on the legacy path never saw their results via the public URL — a bug present since BACKBONE-04 step (a) shipped. PR-X4a writes the terminal row in one shot: `status='published'` plus `metrics_snapshot` built from the Python `results` blob (with `matched_strategy_id` folded in since it isn't a first-class column on `strategy_verifications`). The unified `unifiedVerifyStrategyHandler` is unchanged in this PR — PR-X5 will do the real unified-path build-out for teaser inside `/process-key`. PR-X4a exists separately because the legacy path doubles as the kill-switch auto-rollback target; even after PR-X5 unifies teaser, a rollback must still surface metrics on the SV row.

### Fixed

- **Legacy teaser SV upsert** (`src/app/api/verify-strategy/route.ts`). Status flipped to `'published'` and `metrics_snapshot: { ...analyticsResult.results, matched_strategy_id }` added. `analyticsResult` typing extended to include `results` and `matched_strategy_id` so the TS side matches the post-PR-X2 Python response shape.

### Added

- **2 PR-X4a regression tests** (`tests/integration/phase-19-pra-write.test.ts`). `upserts metrics_snapshot from the Python results blob` asserts the full `{twr, sharpe, return_24h, return_mtd, return_ytd, equity_curve, trade_count, matched_strategy_id}` snapshot lands. `upserts metrics_snapshot=null when Python returns no results blob` covers the defensive edge case (shouldn't happen post-PR-X2, but null-safe regardless). Existing `upserts a complete strategy_verifications row` test updated to assert `status='published'`.

### Notes

The previous PR-X4 (#160) walked back the unified path for teaser entirely. That was the wrong direction — the unified backbone was supposed to handle teaser per `.planning/phases/19-unified-backbone-conditional-on-day-2-gate-commit/19-04-process-key-router-PLAN.md:19`, but the `/process-key` implementation never built the teaser-specific branch. PR-X4 has been closed unmerged; PR-X4a (this PR) ships only the metrics fix as a standalone safety net for the rollback target. PR-X5 will build the actual missing piece — the teaser branch inside `/process-key` plus a sentinel `teaser-anchor` strategy migration to avoid the existing privacy-leak strategy_id hack.

## [0.22.34.1] - 2026-05-14

**phase-19 pre-PR-D prep (PR-X3 of 3).** Fixes a P0 bug in the unified-backbone teaser handler discovered during the abortive 2026-05-14T19:00 kill-switch flip. The TS `unifiedVerifyStrategyHandler` at `src/app/api/verify-strategy/route.ts` forwarded the request body verbatim as `context` without setting `step='validate'`. The `/process-key` validator at `analytics-service/routers/process_key.py:568` rejects every teaser submission with `MISSING_STRATEGY_ID` (422) because the teaser flow has no caller-owned `strategy_id` (the user is testing keys against the universe of strategies — no strategy exists yet). When the kill-switch was flipped ON, the landing-page submit was broken for 3m43s before the bug was caught and the flag was rolled back. PR-X3 ships the 1-line `context: { ...body, step: "validate" }` fix and an integration regression test that asserts `body.context.step === 'validate'` for the teaser flow.

### Fixed

- **`unifiedVerifyStrategyHandler` forwards `context.step='validate'`** (`src/app/api/verify-strategy/route.ts`). Mirrors the contract that `strategies/csv-validate` and `keys/validate-and-encrypt` already satisfy. The fix only changes the unified path — the legacy `legacyVerifyStrategyHandler` is unchanged.

### Added

- **PR-X3 regression test** (`tests/integration/process-key-thin-adapters.test.ts`). Asserts `body.context.step === 'validate'` and `body.context.{api_key,api_secret,exchange}` still pass through. Verified meaningful by stripping the fix and confirming the assertion fails (`expected undefined to be 'validate'`); re-applying the fix returns the test to green.

## [0.22.34.0] - 2026-05-14

### Fixed

- **Reconstruct lock — same-worker serialization for `reconstruct_positions`** (audit-2026-05-07 P1101 caller follow-up). The analytics worker now serializes concurrent same-worker calls to `reconstruct_positions(strategy_id, supabase)` via a per-strategy `asyncio.Lock` registry, closing the watchdog-reclaim-vs-scheduled-tick race where two coroutines could fire the atomic-rebuild RPC for the same strategy concurrently. Cross-process serialization remains the SQL-side `pg_advisory_xact_lock` (migration 113) + `(strategy_id, symbol, side, opened_at)` UNIQUE constraint (migration 119) — the new in-memory lock is defense-in-depth above the SQL boundary, not a replacement.
- **Lock lifecycle hardening** — `_lock_for` uses `setdefault` to eliminate any TOCTOU window on lazy registry init; `strategy_id` is normalized via `str().strip()` so UUID, str, and padded-str representations of the same strategy share one lock bucket; contention logging is gated on actual wait time (>1s threshold via `time.monotonic`) and emitted as `logger.warning` post-acquire, so routine sub-RTT contention doesn't flood log shipping and the recorded wait time is the real one.

### Added

- **Four load-bearing regression tests** in `TestReconstructIdempotency` (analytics-service/tests/test_position_reconstruction.py): same-strategy serialization (max-in-flight == 1), per-strategy concurrency (max-in-inner == 2 — proves keying), lock release on exception, lock release on `CancelledError`. Each empirically verified to fail under a targeted production regression (remove `async with`, share a global lock, replace `async with` with bare `acquire()`).
- **Autouse `_reset_reconstruct_locks` fixture** so module-level `_RECONSTRUCT_LOCKS` state cannot leak across tests under pytest-asyncio's function-scope event loop.

## [0.22.33.0] - 2026-05-14

### Added

- **Allocator role gate (`withAllocatorAuth`)** — new shared route helper enforces `profiles.role IN ('allocator','both')` on top of `withAuth`. Three failure modes are distinct (instead of one lying 403): DB lookup error → 503 + Sentry breadcrumb, missing profile row → 403 "Profile not provisioned", role mismatch → 403 "allocator role required". Wired into `/api/allocator/scenario/commit` and `/api/strategies/browse`. A branded `AllocatorUser` type prevents accidental downgrade to `withAuth` via the compiler (P1946).
- **Idempotency-Key support on `POST /api/allocator/scenario/commit`** — RFC draft-ietf-httpapi-idempotency-key. Retried requests with the same key return the cached commit envelope without re-recording match decisions. Reservation happens inside the SQL function (migration 131) in the same transaction as the data inserts, so concurrent retries serialize via `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` row-lock — no double-commit under any race. New error codes returned by the function map to HTTP statuses: `idempotency_body_mismatch` → 422 (key reused with different body, per RFC §2.5), `idempotency_in_flight` → 409 + `Retry-After: 1`, `idempotency_schema_drift` → 503. Key validation restricted to ASCII alphanumerics + URL-safe punctuation (16-128 chars) so JS `.length` and Postgres `length(text)` agree by construction (P1945).
- **Body-hash binding on cached idempotency rows** — SHA-256 of the raw request body is stored alongside each cached response. Retries with the same key but a different body return 422 instead of silently serving the original body. Cached rows also carry a `schema_version` so a future shape change automatically invalidates stale entries.
- **`Cache-Control: private, no-store` on every authenticated response path** — including the 401 from `withAuth`, the three 403 paths in `assertSameOrigin` (CSRF), and every status code from both routes (200/400/422/429/500/503). Prevents intermediary caches from serving authenticated payloads cross-tenant (P1947).
- **Shared helpers extracted** — `src/lib/api/headers.ts` (single source of truth for `NO_STORE_HEADERS`) and `src/lib/sentry-capture.ts` (lazy-import Sentry helper, previously duplicated across `admin.ts`, `audit.ts`, `withAllocatorAuth.ts`, and `commit/route.ts`). Future audits adding `Vary` or `Pragma` headers land in one file.

### Changed

- **`/api/allocator/scenario/commit` returns HTTP 422 on full rollback** — was 200 with `recorded: 0`. Legacy clients that watched only the status code couldn't distinguish a rolled-back batch from a successful one; the body shape (`recorded: 0, results: [], errors: [...]`) is unchanged so the drawer UI still renders inline failures.
- **`commit_scenario_batch` RPC signature** — added `p_idempotency_key text` and `p_request_hash text` parameters (both default NULL, callers without idempotency unchanged). Reservation + per-row inserts + final cache-write all run in one Postgres transaction; rollback rolls back the reservation, so retries proceed fresh.
- **`stampOutcomeMarker` runs in `after()`** — first-outcome stamp now executes post-response via Next.js `after()`, dropping two Auth Admin REST round-trips off p95 commit latency. A try/`queueMicrotask` fallback mirrors `src/lib/audit.ts` so non-request scopes (vitest, cron) still work.
- **Audit metadata carries `commit_batch_id`** — each batch generates a v4 UUID emitted on every `match.decision_record` audit row. Forensic SQL `GROUP BY commit_batch_id` clusters per-row events for one logical commit; if the cache write ever fails and a retry duplicates writes, the duplicates share an id.

### Fixed

- **Migration 130 is now idempotent** — earlier revisions of the same file would have left environments stuck on a stale schema if they had applied an older version. `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` plus `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` (with backfill + `SET NOT NULL` + named CHECK constraint) makes re-application safe across all prior states.

## [0.22.32.0] - 2026-05-14

### Fixed

- **MetricPanel Trade Metrics chip — round-2-F audit hardening (P2035)** — The Trade Metrics chip on the strategy detail page now reads real fields off `TradeMetrics` and renders honestly when the data is incomplete. Six issues addressed in one round:
  - **Partial 4-bucket payloads no longer fabricate a confident Maker Share** — the gate previously accepted any payload with `long_maker` OR `short_maker` defined, so a producer drift emitting only `long_maker` would render "+100.00% Maker Share" from incomplete data. Now requires all four buckets (`long_maker`, `long_taker`, `short_maker`, `short_taker`) before computing — partial mixes hide the row.
  - **Total Positions = 0 renders `0` literally** — pre-fix the chip rendered "—" for both "no positions yet" and "missing data", merging two operationally distinct states into one indistinguishable glyph. New strategies with zero closed positions now show "0"; "—" is reserved for genuinely-nullish values.
  - **Dropped the silent `as TradeMetrics | null` cast** on `a.trade_metrics` — the source field is already typed; the cast was papering over any future widening (the same class of bug that produced P2035 originally).
  - **Type-guard now pins `trade_mix` key + field widths** — `MetricPanel.types.test.ts` previously only pinned field names. A widening of `total_positions`/`long_count`/`win_rate` from `number` to `number | null` would have slipped past. New width pins (`extends number ? true : false`) fail `tsc --noEmit` on any drift.
  - **Stable `data-testid` on accordion wrapper** — replaces brittle `heading.parentElement` test scoping. Survives DOM-structure refactors.
  - **Accordion accessibility** — added `aria-expanded` / `aria-controls` to the toggle button, `role="region"` + `aria-label` to the body, and `aria-hidden="true"` to the chevron. Pre-existing gap surfaced by the design specialist; fixed while touching the wrapper.

### Changed

- **MetricPanel test fixtures consolidated** — five inline 17-field `TradeMetrics` literals replaced with a shared `makeTradeMetrics(overrides?)` helper mirroring the existing `makeAnalytics` pattern. Test bodies dropped from ~80 lines of filler to 2–4 fields each. Future `TradeMetrics` schema changes need updating in one place, not five.

## [0.22.31.0] - 2026-05-14

### Fixed

- **Scenario commit drawer — backdrop + X button now blocked during submit (audit-2026-05-07 round-2-C)** — The Esc-during-submit guard added in round-2-B only covered the keyboard path; backdrop click and the header "×" button still called `onClose` unconditionally. Clicking either mid-submit silently aborted the in-flight commit and left the allocator wondering whether the real-money batch had landed.
